rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Leafeon

cards.flavorText: The younger they are, the more they smell like fresh grass. With age, their fragrance takes on the odor of fallen leaves.

cards.name: Glaceon

attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, put 9 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.

cards.name: Sylveon

attacks.name: Mystical Return

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: Sylveon cuts an elegant figure as it dances lightly around, feelers fluttering, but its piercing moves aim straight for its opponents' weak spots.

cards.name: Eevee

abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, it can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

cards.flavorText: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

cards.name: Eevee ex

abilities.name: Rainbow DNA
abilities.text: This Pokémon can evolve into any Pokémon ex that evolves from Eevee if you play it from your hand onto this Pokémon. (This Pokémon can't evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.)

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Yanmega

attacks.name: Gyro Shockwave
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its jaw power is incredible. It is adept at biting apart foes while flying by at high speed.

attacks.text: Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of your Benched Pokémon and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: As halfhearted as this Pokémon's kicks may seem, they pack enough power to shatter Conkeldurr's concrete pillars.

cards.name: Koraidon ex

attacks.name: Revenge Buster
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 120 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Metal Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hammer Whip
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It's incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

cards.name: Eevee

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It can evolve into a variety of forms. Eevee's genes are the key to solving the mysteries of Pokémon evolution.

cards.name: Zebstrika

attacks.name: Full Speed
attacks.text: Discard your hand and draw 6 cards.

cards.flavorText: Once the herd hears thunder, it chases after the storm clouds so that the Blitzle in the group can use the lightning to charge up.

cards.name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Although it's carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.

cards.name: Sprigatito ex

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Feraligatr

attacks.name: Giant Wave
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Giant Wave.

abilities.name: Torrential Heart
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 5 damage counters on this Pokémon. If you do, during this turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

cards.name: Metang

abilities.name: Metal Maker
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 4 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic Metal Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck.

cards.flavorText: Two Beldum have become stuck together via their own magnetic forces. With two brains, the resulting Metang has doubled psychic powers.

cards.name: Koraidon

attacks.name: Primordial Beatdown
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Ancient Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

cards.name: Miraidon

attacks.name: Peak Acceleration
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Future Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

cards.name: Flutter Mane

attacks.name: Hex Hurl
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

abilities.name: Midnight Fluttering
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Active Pokémon has no Abilities, except for Midnight Fluttering.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

cards.name: Iron Thorns

attacks.name: Destructo-Press
attacks.text: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 70 damage for each Future card you find there. Then, discard those Future cards and shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

cards.flavorText: Some of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.

attacks.name: Armor Cannon
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

rules: Flip a coin. If heads, draw 4 cards. If tails, draw 2 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Thwackey

abilities.name: Boom Boom Groove
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if your Active Pokémon has the Festival Lead Ability, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

cards.name: Infernape

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It is beaten by none in terms of quickness.

cards.name: Froslass

abilities.text: During Pokémon Checkup, put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon that has an Ability (both yours and your opponent's), except any Froslass.

cards.flavorText: It freezes prey by blowing its −58 degree Fahrenheit breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

cards.name: Tatsugiri

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal a Supporter card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

cards.flavorText: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

cards.name: Snorlax
cards.flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

cards.name: Teal Mask Ogerpon

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike.

cards.flavorText: The sharp scythes on its forearms become increasingly sharp by cutting through hard objects.

cards.name: Pineco
cards.flavorText: It spits out a fluid that it uses to glue tree bark to its body. The fluid hardens when it touches air.

cards.name: Seedot

attacks.name: Rigidify
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: If it remains still, it looks just like a real nut. It delights in surprising foraging Pokémon.

cards.name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Comet Slap
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

cards.name: Shiftry

attacks.name: Expelling Tornado
attacks.text: Choose 3 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If you do, shuffle all of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that you didn't choose, and all cards attached to those Pokémon, into their deck.

attacks.name: Energy Loop
attacks.text: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

cards.flavorText: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe's mind and take preemptive action.

cards.name: Shroomish

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: If it senses danger, it scatters spores from the top of its head to protect itself.

cards.name: Breloom

attacks.name: Knuckle Impact

cards.flavorText: The seeds on its tail are made of toxic spores. It knocks out foes with quick, virtually invisible punches.

cards.name: Roselia

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

cards.name: Roserade

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

cards.name: Turtwig

abilities.name: Solid Shell
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Photosynthesis occurs across its body under the sun. The shell on its back is actually hardened soil.

cards.name: Grotle

attacks.name: Ramming Shell
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

cards.name: Torterra ex

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Grass Pokémon in play.

attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.name: Shaymin

cards.flavorText: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

cards.name: Cottonee

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

cards.name: Whimsicott

abilities.name: Wafting Heal
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal all damage from your Active Grass Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, discard all Energy from that Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it'll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

cards.name: Deerling

attacks.name: Leaf Litter Tackle
attacks.text: Discard a Grass Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Deerling have different scents depending on the season. In this form, Deerling have a softly sweet scent that lingers in the nose.

cards.name: Sawsbuck

abilities.name: Changing Seasons
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Sawsbuck in their Autumn Form are known for having very short tempers. The males fight each other incessantly.

cards.name: Grubbin

attacks.name: Flock
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Grubbin and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Its natural enemies, like Rookidee, may flee rather than risk getting caught in its large mandibles that can snap thick tree branches.

cards.name: Dhelmise

attacks.name: Steel Anchor
attacks.text: If you have any Metal Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.flavorText: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

cards.name: Bramblin
cards.flavorText: Not even Bramblin knows where it is headed as it tumbles across the wilderness, blown by the wind. It loathes getting wet.

cards.name: Brambleghast

cards.flavorText: Brambleghast wanders around arid regions. On rare occasions, mass outbreaks of these Pokémon will bury an entire town.

abilities.text: Prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

abilities.name: Rapid Vernier
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, you may move any amount of Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Charge Energy
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It can't run properly when it's newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

cards.name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Inferno Onrush
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

cards.name: Slugma

attacks.name: Roasting Heat
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 40 more damage.

cards.flavorText: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

cards.name: Magcargo

abilities.name: Lava Zone
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent's Active Pokémon moves to the Bench during their turn, their new Active Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

cards.name: Victini

attacks.name: Flippity Flap
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Singe Off
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

cards.name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Licking Flames
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each tails, discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: There's a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

cards.name: Litten

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It spends even the smallest amount of downtime grooming its fur with its tongue. Loose fur gathers in its stomach and serves as fuel for fiery moves.

cards.name: Torracat

attacks.name: Flare Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Flare Strike.

cards.flavorText: In the midst of battle, the fire pouch on Torracat's neck rings like a bell and produces stronger flames than usual.

cards.name: Incineroar ex

attacks.name: Blaze Blast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

abilities.name: Hustle Play
abilities.text: Attacks used by this Pokémon cost Colorless less for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Turtonator

attacks.name: Spit-Out Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

cards.name: Sizzlipede

attacks.name: Heat Dive
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

cards.name: Centiskorch

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Charring Breath
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't Burned, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

cards.name: Gouging Fire ex

attacks.name: Blaze Blitz
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Blaze Blitz again until it leaves the Active Spot.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Totodile

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: It is small but rough and tough. It won't hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

cards.name: Croconaw

attacks.name: Reverse Thrust
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

cards.name: Feraligatr

attacks.name: Giant Wave
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Giant Wave.

abilities.name: Torrential Heart
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 5 damage counters on this Pokémon. If you do, during this turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

cards.name: Carvanha
cards.flavorText: It won't attack while it's alone—not even if it spots prey. Instead, it waits for other Carvanha to join it, and then the Pokémon attack as a group.

cards.name: Sharpedo

attacks.name: Chew Off
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: As soon as it catches the scent of prey, Sharpedo will jet seawater from its backside, hurtling toward the target to attack at 75 mph.

cards.name: Keldeo

attacks.name: Rapid Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

cards.flavorText: Keldeo has strengthened its resolve for battle, filling its body with power and changing its form.

cards.name: Snom

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

cards.flavorText: It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

cards.name: Frosmoth

attacks.name: Freezing Chill
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Frosmoth senses air currents with its antennae. It sends its scales drifting on frigid air, making them fall like snow.

cards.name: Wiglett

attacks.name: Lucky Find
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Aqua Bomb
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

cards.name: Finizen
cards.flavorText: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

cards.name: Palafin

attacks.name: Vanguard Punch
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon's ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.

cards.name: Walking Wake ex

attacks.name: Catharsis Roar
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.

abilities.name: Azure Seas
abilities.text: Damage from attacks used by this Pokémon isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

cards.name: Raichu

attacks.name: Collateral Bolts
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each Pokémon that has any damage counters on it (both yours and your opponent's), except for this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.

cards.name: Electabuzz
cards.flavorText: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

cards.name: Electivire

attacks.name: Short-Circuit Knuckle
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Water Pokémon in play, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

cards.name: Charjabug
cards.flavorText: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

cards.name: Vikavolt

attacks.name: Circuit Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 80 more damage for each of your Benched Charjabug.

cards.flavorText: It builds up electricity in its abdomen, focuses it through its jaws, and then fires the electricity off in concentrated beams.

cards.name: Zeraora

attacks.name: Shocking Knuckle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Strong Volt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.

cards.name: Yamper

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

cards.name: Boltund

attacks.name: Electrifying Dash
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Lightning Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

cards.name: Wugtrio ex

attacks.name: Tricolor Pump
attacks.text: Discard up to 3 Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy card you discarded in this way. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Numbing Hold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Iron Hands

attacks.name: Volt Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Superalloy Hands
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon shares many similarities with Iron Hands, an object mentioned in a certain expedition journal.

cards.name: Iron Thorns

attacks.name: Destructo-Press
attacks.text: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 70 damage for each Future card you find there. Then, discard those Future cards and shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

cards.flavorText: Some of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.

cards.name: Mr. Mime

attacks.name: Look-Alike Show
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may use the effect of a Supporter card you find there as the effect of this attack.

attacks.name: Eerie Wave
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

cards.name: Marill

attacks.name: Ball Roll
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry even when it plays in the water.

cards.name: Azumarill

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Power Tackle
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of things in water and tell what they are.

cards.name: Girafarig

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Girafarig's tail has a small head. It instinctively bites at any foe that approaches the Pokémon from behind.

cards.name: Latias

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

cards.flavorText: It communicates using telepathy. Its body is covered in down that refracts light to make it invisible.

cards.name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Mirror Attack
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Psychic Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor's back contained a mysterious power.

cards.name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Evolution Jammer
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

cards.name: Solosis

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: It communicates with others telepathically. Its body is encapsulated in liquid, but if it takes a heavy blow, the liquid will leak out.

cards.name: Duosion

attacks.name: Double Trick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Its psychic power can supposedly cover a range of more than half a mile—but only if its two brains can agree with each other.

cards.name: Reuniclus

attacks.name: Summoning Gate
attacks.text: Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. You may put any number of Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Brain Shake
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: While it could use its psychic abilities in battle, this Pokémon prefers to swing its powerful arms around to beat opponents into submission.

cards.name: Elgyem

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: If this Pokémon stands near a TV, strange scenery will appear on the screen. That scenery is said to be from its home.

cards.name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Cosmic Beatdown
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.

cards.flavorText: Whenever a Beheeyem visits a farm, a Dubwool mysteriously disappears.

cards.name: Cutiefly

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. You can find Cutiefly hovering around Gossifleur, trying to get some of Gossifleur's pollen.

cards.name: Ribombee

attacks.name: Plentiful Pollen
attacks.text: During your next turn, if the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out, take 2 more Prize cards.

cards.flavorText: It makes pollen puffs from pollen and nectar. The puffs' effects depend on the type of ingredients and how much of each one is used.

cards.name: Scream Tail

attacks.name: Supportive Singing
attacks.text: Heal 100 damage from 1 of your Benched Ancient Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

cards.name: Flutter Mane

attacks.name: Hex Hurl
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

abilities.name: Midnight Fluttering
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Active Pokémon has no Abilities, except for Midnight Fluttering.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

cards.name: Iron Valiant

attacks.name: Gemini Laser
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Zen Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Zen Blade.

cards.flavorText: It's possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

cards.name: Iron Valiant

attacks.name: Calculation
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.

attacks.name: Majestic Sword
attacks.text: If you played a Future Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It's possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

cards.name: Iron Crown ex

attacks.name: Twin Shotels
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on those Pokémon.

abilities.name: Cobalt Command
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Future Pokémon, except any Iron Crown ex, do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Meditite
cards.flavorText: It meditates to heighten its inner energy and to float in the air. It eats one berry a day.

cards.name: Medicham
cards.flavorText: It elegantly avoids attacks with dance-like steps, then launches a devastating blow in the same motion.

cards.name: Relicanth

abilities.name: Memory Dive
abilities.text: Each of your evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

cards.flavorText: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

cards.name: Drilbur

abilities.name: Dig Dig Dig
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 Basic Fighting Energy cards and discard them. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It brings its claws together and whirls around at high speed before rushing toward its prey.

cards.name: Excadrill

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It's not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill's rampant digging.

cards.name: Golett

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

cards.flavorText: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

cards.name: Golurk

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

attacks.name: Fighting Fist
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.

cards.name: Rockruff
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is very friendly when it's young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

cards.name: Lycanroc

attacks.name: Turbo Edge
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

cards.flavorText: Lycanroc attacks its prey with its sharp claws and fangs. It loyally obeys the instructions of a Trainer it trusts.

cards.name: Mudbray
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon covers itself in mud that it has regurgitated. The mud won't dry out even if it's exposed to the sun for a long time.

cards.name: Mudsdale

attacks.name: Mud Stock
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: High Horsepower
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

cards.name: Rolycoly
cards.flavorText: On sunny days, Rolycoly will come out onto grassy plains and roll around. Then it will eat the grass that gets tangled in its wheel-like leg.

cards.name: Carkol

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The temperature inside its body increases when it experiences strong emotions. It rolls around frantically while spewing flames.

cards.name: Coalossal

attacks.name: Gatling Tar
attacks.text: This attack does 80 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cragalanche
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: To intimidate its opponents, Coalossal will vigorously shake its body, scattering coal from its smoldering back.

cards.name: Great Tusk

attacks.name: Wrathful Charge
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.

cards.name: Great Tusk

attacks.name: Land Collapse
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck. If you played an Ancient Supporter card from your hand during this turn, discard 3 more cards in this way.

cards.flavorText: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.

cards.name: Sandy Shocks

attacks.name: Magnetic Burst
attacks.text: If you have 3 or more Energy in play, this attack does 70 more damage. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

cards.flavorText: No records exist of this Pokémon being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon's traits match up with a creature shown in an expedition journal.

cards.name: Iron Boulder ex

attacks.name: Repulsor Axe
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Power Stomp
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Ekans

attacks.name: Poison Blend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: The older it gets, the longer it grows. At night, it wraps its long body around tree branches to rest.

cards.name: Arbok

attacks.name: Panic Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: The pattern on its belly appears to be a frightening face. Weak foes will flee just at the sight of the pattern.

cards.name: Gastly

attacks.name: Mysterious Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

cards.name: Haunter

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

cards.name: Gengar ex

attacks.name: Tricky Steps
attacks.text: You may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Gnawing Curse
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Poochyena

attacks.name: Gnaw Off
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

cards.name: Mightyena

attacks.name: Kick Away
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

cards.name: Sableye

attacks.name: Damage Collection
attacks.text: You may move any number of damage counters from your opponent's Benched Pokémon to their Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

cards.name: Farigiraf ex

attacks.name: Dirty Beam
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Armor Tail
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Basic Pokémon ex.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Roaring Moon

attacks.name: Vengeance Fletching
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Ancient card in your discard pile.

cards.flavorText: It is possible that this is the creature listed as Roaring Moon in an expedition journal that still holds many mysteries.

cards.name: Forretress

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Steel Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

cards.name: Scizor ex

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Cross Breaker
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Metal Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Mawile

attacks.name: Invite and Strike
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

cards.name: Beldum

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: From its rear, Beldum emits a magnetic force that rapidly pulls opponents in. They get skewered on Beldum's sharp claws.

cards.name: Metang

abilities.name: Metal Maker
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 4 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic Metal Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck.

cards.flavorText: Two Beldum have become stuck together via their own magnetic forces. With two brains, the resulting Metang has doubled psychic powers.

cards.name: Metagross

attacks.name: Meteor Mash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Meteor Mash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Luster Blast
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Because the magnetic powers of these Pokémon get stronger in freezing temperatures, Metagross living on snowy mountains are full of energy.

cards.name: Meltan
cards.flavorText: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

cards.name: Melmetal
cards.flavorText: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.

cards.name: Iron Treads

attacks.name: Wheel Pass
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Dual Core
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon has a Future Booster Energy Capsule attached, it is Fighting and Metal type.

cards.flavorText: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. It resembles a mysterious object described in an old expedition journal.

cards.name: Koraidon

attacks.name: Primordial Beatdown
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Ancient Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

cards.name: Koraidon ex

attacks.name: Retribution Strike
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Kaiser Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Miraidon

attacks.name: Peak Acceleration
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Future Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

cards.name: Miraidon ex

attacks.name: Repulsion Bolt
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Cyber Drive
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Cyber Drive.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Raging Bolt ex

attacks.name: Burst Roar
attacks.text: Discard your hand and draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Bellowing Thunder
attacks.text: You may discard any amount of Basic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Tongue Pull
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Put up to 2 Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent's Bench.

cards.flavorText: If this Pokémon's sticky saliva gets on you and you don't clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won't go away, either.

cards.name: Lickilicky

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Tonguenado
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Lickilicky's strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky's tongue can stretch so far.

cards.name: Hoothoot

attacks.name: Silent Wing
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

cards.flavorText: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

cards.name: Noctowl

attacks.name: Talon Hunt
attacks.text: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

cards.name: Dunsparce

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It creates mazes in dark locations. When spotted, it flees into the ground by digging with its tail.

cards.name: Dudunsparce

abilities.name: Run Away Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.

cards.name: Skitty

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It can't stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

cards.name: Delcatty

attacks.name: Tail Trickery
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Energy Blender
attacks.text: You may move any amount of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

cards.flavorText: It dislikes dirty places. It often searches for a comfortable place in which to groom itself.

cards.name: Chatot

attacks.name: A Cappella
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it's one of them. This way they won't attack it.

cards.name: Pidove

abilities.name: Emergency Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon's remaining HP is 30 or less, you may search your deck for an Unfezant or Unfezant ex and put it onto this Pidove to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Where people go, these Pokémon follow. If you're scattering food for them, be careful— several hundred of them can gather at once.

cards.name: Tranquill

attacks.name: Jet Wing
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: It can fly moderately quickly. No matter how far it travels, it can always find its way back to its master and its nest.

cards.name: Unfezant

attacks.name: Opposing Winds
attacks.text: You may put 2 Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Unfezant are exceptional fliers. The females are known for their stamina, while the males outclass them in terms of speed.

cards.name: Minccino

attacks.name: Cleaning Up
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

cards.name: Cinccino

attacks.name: Special Roll
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Special Energy card attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

cards.name: Drampa

attacks.name: Raging Cannon
attacks.text: If all of your Benched Pokémon have at least 1 damage counter on them, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

cards.name: Iron Jugulis

abilities.name: Automated Combat
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It's possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.

cards.name: Ancient Booster Energy Capsule
rules: The Ancient Pokémon this card is attached to gets +60 HP, recovers from all Special Conditions, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Awakening Drum
rules: Draw a card for each of your Ancient Pokémon in play.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Bianca's Devotion
rules: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has 30 HP or less remaining.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Boxed Order
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Buddy-Buddy Poffin
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Ciphermaniac's Codebreaking
rules: Search your deck for 2 cards, shuffle your deck, then put those cards on top of it in any order.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Eri
rules: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you discard up to 2 Item cards you find there.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Explorer's Guidance
rules: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Full Metal Lab
rules: Metal Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) take 30 less damage from attacks from the opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it into the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Future Booster Energy Capsule
rules: The Future Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost, and the attacks it uses do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Hand Trimmer
rules: Each player discards cards from their hand until they have 5 cards in their hand. Your opponent discards first. (If a player has 5 or fewer cards in their hand, they do not discard.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Heavy Baton
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has a Retreat Cost of exactly 4, is in the Active Spot, and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move up to 3 Basic Energy cards from that Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Hero's Cape
rules: The Pokémon this card is attached to gets +100 HP.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Master Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Maximum Belt
rules: Attacks used by the Pokémon this card is attached to do 50 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Morty's Conviction
rules: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.  Draw a card for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Perilous Jungle
rules: During Pokémon Checkup, put 2 more damage counters on each Poisoned non-Darkness Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it into the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Prime Catcher
rules: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Reboot Pod
rules: Attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of your Future Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Rescue Board
rules: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is Colorless less. If that Pokémon's remaining HP is 30 or less, it has no Retreat Cost.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Salvatore
rules: Search your deck for a card that has no Abilities and evolves from 1 of your Pokémon, and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. You can use this card on a Pokémon you put down when you were setting up to play or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Mist Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.    Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to the Pokémon this card is attached to. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)

cards.name: Neo Upper Energy
rules: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  If this card is attached to a Stage 2 Pokémon, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 2 Energy at a time.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Shiftry

attacks.name: Expelling Tornado
attacks.text: Choose 3 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If you do, shuffle all of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that you didn't choose, and all cards attached to those Pokémon, into their deck.

attacks.name: Energy Loop
attacks.text: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

cards.flavorText: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe's mind and take preemptive action.

cards.name: Grotle

attacks.name: Ramming Shell
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

cards.name: Deerling

attacks.name: Leaf Litter Tackle
attacks.text: Discard a Grass Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Deerling have different scents depending on the season. In this form, Deerling have a softly sweet scent that lingers in the nose.

cards.name: Sawsbuck

abilities.name: Changing Seasons
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Sawsbuck in their Autumn Form are known for having very short tempers. The males fight each other incessantly.

cards.name: Litten

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It spends even the smallest amount of downtime grooming its fur with its tongue. Loose fur gathers in its stomach and serves as fuel for fiery moves.

cards.name: Snom

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

cards.flavorText: It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

cards.name: Charjabug
cards.flavorText: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

cards.name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Mirror Attack
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Psychic Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor's back contained a mysterious power.

cards.name: Reuniclus

attacks.name: Summoning Gate
attacks.text: Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. You may put any number of Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Brain Shake
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: While it could use its psychic abilities in battle, this Pokémon prefers to swing its powerful arms around to beat opponents into submission.

cards.name: Cutiefly

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. You can find Cutiefly hovering around Gossifleur, trying to get some of Gossifleur's pollen.

cards.name: Relicanth

abilities.name: Memory Dive
abilities.text: Each of your evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

cards.flavorText: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

cards.name: Excadrill

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It's not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill's rampant digging.

cards.name: Mudsdale

attacks.name: Mud Stock
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: High Horsepower
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

cards.name: Arbok

attacks.name: Panic Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: The pattern on its belly appears to be a frightening face. Weak foes will flee just at the sight of the pattern.

cards.name: Gastly

attacks.name: Mysterious Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

cards.name: Metagross

attacks.name: Meteor Mash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Meteor Mash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Luster Blast
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Because the magnetic powers of these Pokémon get stronger in freezing temperatures, Metagross living on snowy mountains are full of energy.

cards.name: Meltan
cards.flavorText: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

cards.name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Tongue Pull
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Put up to 2 Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent's Bench.

cards.flavorText: If this Pokémon's sticky saliva gets on you and you don't clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won't go away, either.

cards.name: Chatot

attacks.name: A Cappella
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it's one of them. This way they won't attack it.

attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Special Energy card attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

attacks.text: If all of your Benched Pokémon have at least 1 damage counter on them, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

attacks.name: Forest March
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Grass Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Jungle Hammer
attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Prism Edge
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, you may move any amount of Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Incineroar ex

attacks.name: Blaze Blast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

abilities.name: Hustle Play
abilities.text: Attacks used by this Pokémon cost Colorless less for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blaze Blitz
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Blaze Blitz again until it leaves the Active Spot.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Catharsis Roar
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.

abilities.name: Azure Seas
abilities.text: Damage from attacks used by this Pokémon isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Wugtrio ex

attacks.name: Tricolor Pump
attacks.text: Discard up to 3 Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy card you discarded in this way. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Numbing Hold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
