rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Gouging Fire ex

attacks.name: Blaze Blitz
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Blaze Blitz again until it leaves the Active Spot.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Walking Wake ex

attacks.name: Catharsis Roar
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.

abilities.name: Azure Seas
abilities.text: Damage from attacks used by this Pokémon isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Iron Crown ex

attacks.name: Twin Shotels
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on those Pokémon.

abilities.name: Cobalt Command
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Future Pokémon, except any Iron Crown ex, do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Iron Boulder ex

attacks.name: Repulsor Axe
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Power Stomp
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Raging Bolt ex

attacks.name: Burst Roar
attacks.text: Discard your hand and draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Bellowing Thunder
attacks.text: You may discard any amount of Basic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Tangela
cards.flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon's true appearance remains a mystery.

cards.name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Loom Over
attacks.text: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Thicket Body
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

cards.name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Slow Crunch
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

cards.name: Spinarak
cards.flavorText: Some fishers weave its sturdy thread into nets to catch fish Pokémon.

cards.name: Ariados

attacks.name: String Bind
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

abilities.name: Big Net
abilities.text: Your opponent's Active Evolution Pokémon's Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

cards.flavorText: It wanders in search of food after darkness falls, never nesting in a specific place.

cards.name: Sunkern

attacks.name: Bullet Seed
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It is very weak. Its only means of defense is to shake its leaves desperately at its attacker.

cards.name: Sunflora

attacks.name: Redirected Sunlight
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Fire Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Since it converts sunlight into energy, it is always looking in the direction of the sun.

cards.name: Heracross

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: With its herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.

cards.name: Volbeat

attacks.name: Quick Sign
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Coordinated Strike
attacks.text: If Illumise is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It flashes the light on its rear to communicate with other Volbeat. It loves the sweet aroma given off by Illumise.

cards.name: Illumise

attacks.name: Slowing Perfume
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Shuffle 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.

cards.flavorText: It guides Volbeat to draw signs in the night sky. There are scholars who research the meaning of these signs.

cards.name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Leaflet Blessings
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal all damage from that Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The younger they are, the more they smell like fresh grass. With age, their fragrance takes on the odor of fallen leaves.

cards.name: Phantump

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon came to be when the spirit of a deceased child possessed a tree stump. It now wanders the forest searching for friends.

cards.name: Trevenant

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Trevenant is very kind to Pokémon living in the forest. It doesn't even care if these Pokémon take up residence in the greenery on its head.

cards.name: Grookey
cards.flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

cards.name: Thwackey

abilities.name: Boom Boom Groove
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if your Active Pokémon has the Festival Lead Ability, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

cards.name: Rillaboom

attacks.name: Drum Beating
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Wood Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

cards.name: Applin

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

cards.name: Dipplin

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Festival Lead
abilities.text: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

cards.name: Iron Leaves

attacks.name: Recovery Net
attacks.text: Put up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Avenging Edge
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: According to the few eyewitness accounts that exist, it used its shining blades to julienne large trees and boulders.

cards.name: Poltchageist

abilities.name: Storehouse Hideaway
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

cards.name: Poltchageist

attacks.name: Tea Server
attacks.text: Put a Basic Grass Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

cards.name: Sinistcha

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Spill the Tea
attacks.text: Discard up to 3 Grass Energy cards from your Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

cards.flavorText: It prefers cool, dark places, such as the back of a shelf or the space beneath a home's floorboards. It wanders in search of prey after sunset.

cards.name: Sinistcha ex

attacks.name: Re-Brew
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Basic Grass Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those Energy cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Matcha Splash
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Teal Mask Ogerpon

attacks.name: Mountain Stroll
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Ogre Comeback
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

cards.name: Teal Mask Ogerpon ex

attacks.name: Myriad Leaf Shower
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Teal Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Grass Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw a card.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Vulpix
cards.flavorText: As its body grows larger, its six warm tails become more beautiful, with a more luxurious coat of fur.

cards.name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Eerie Glow
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

cards.flavorText: It has nine long tails and fur that gleams gold. It is said to live for 1,000 years.

cards.name: Slugma

attacks.name: Hot Magma
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: It's said that in ancient times, when volcanoes were more active, there were many more Slugma than there are now.

cards.name: Magcargo ex

attacks.name: Hot Magma
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Ground Burn
attacks.text: Discard the top card of each player's deck. This attack does 140 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Torkoal

attacks.name: Ramming Shell
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Coal is the source of Torkoal's energy. Large amounts of coal can be found in the mountains where they live.

cards.name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Even rain can't extinguish the fire.

cards.name: Monferno

attacks.name: Heat Blow
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

cards.name: Infernape

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Pyro Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Fire Energy card, a Basic Fighting Energy card, or 1 of each from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

cards.flavorText: Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It is beaten by none in terms of quickness.

cards.name: Darumaka

attacks.name: Fire Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It derives its power from fire burning inside its body. If the fire dwindles, this Pokémon will immediately fall asleep.

cards.name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Inferno Onrush
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: The thick arms of this hot-blooded Pokémon can deliver punches capable of obliterating a dump truck.

cards.name: Litwick

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life-force of any who follow it.

cards.name: Lampent

attacks.name: Burn It All Up
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

cards.name: Chandelure

attacks.name: Mind Ruler
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

abilities.name: Alluring Light
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may have each player draw a card.

cards.flavorText: It absorbs spirits, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

cards.name: Chi-Yu

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Ground Melter
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 60 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.

cards.flavorText: The envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.

cards.name: Hearthflame Mask Ogerpon ex

attacks.name: Wrathful Hearth
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamic Blaze
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage, and discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Poliwag

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Its legs are newly grown, and it can't walk very well. It seems to prefer swimming through the water instead.

cards.name: Poliwhirl

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Its skin is moist all over its body. The skin on its belly spiral also feels smooth.

cards.name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Jumping Uppercut
attacks.text: You may do 120 more damage. If you do, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

cards.flavorText: It can use its well-developed arms and legs to run on the surface of the water for a split second.

cards.name: Goldeen

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Festival Lead
abilities.text: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

cards.name: Seaking

attacks.name: Peck Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

cards.name: Jynx

attacks.name: Inviting Kiss
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you put any Pokémon onto your Bench in this way, move an Energy from this Pokémon to the new Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: In certain parts of Galar, Jynx was once feared and worshiped as the Queen of Ice.

cards.name: Corphish
cards.flavorText: It can live in impure water, where it doesn't need to compete with other water Pokémon for food, so its numbers have steadily increased.

cards.name: Crawdaunt

attacks.name: Snip Snip
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Rampaging Hammer
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: A rough customer that wildly flails its giant claws. It is said to be extremely hard to raise.

cards.name: Feebas

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It is the shabbiest Pokémon of all. It forms schools and lives at the bottom of rivers that teem with plant life.

cards.name: Milotic

abilities.name: Mentally Calm
abilities.text: Your opponent's Pokémon in play and all attached cards can't be put into your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: It lives at the bottom of clear lakes. In times of war, it shows itself, which soothes people's minds and hearts.

cards.name: Snorunt

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

cards.flavorText: It is said that a home visited by a Snorunt will prosper. It can withstand cold of −150 degrees Fahrenheit.

cards.name: Glalie

attacks.name: Damage Beat
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crazy Headbutt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It prevents prey from escaping by instantaneously freezing moisture in the air.

cards.name: Froslass

abilities.name: Freezing Shroud
abilities.text: During Pokémon Checkup, put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon that has an Ability (both yours and your opponent's), except any Froslass.

cards.flavorText: It freezes prey by blowing its −58 degree Fahrenheit breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

cards.name: Glaceon

attacks.name: Permeating Chill
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, put 9 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.

cards.name: Phione

attacks.name: Beckon
attacks.text: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

cards.name: Froakie

attacks.name: Flock
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Froakie and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

cards.name: Frogadier

attacks.name: Numbing Water
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

cards.name: Cramorant

attacks.name: Spit Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It's an incredibly poor learner because it uses the bare minimum amount of energy for its brain. It focuses on battling instead.

cards.name: Finizen

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Its water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.

cards.name: Palafin

abilities.name: Zero to Hero
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from the Active Spot to the Bench, you may search your deck for a Palafin ex and switch it with this Pokémon. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon. If you switched a Pokémon in this way, put this card into your deck. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Its physical capabilities are no different than a Finizen's, but when its allies are in danger, it transforms and powers itself up.

cards.name: Palafin ex

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

abilities.name: Hero's Spirit
abilities.text: Put this Pokémon into play only with the effect of Palafin's Zero to Hero Ability.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Iron Bundle

attacks.name: Interjet
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

cards.name: Walking Wake

attacks.name: Aurora Gain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Undulating Slice
attacks.text: Put up to 9 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.

cards.flavorText: This ferocious creature is shrouded in mystery. It's named after an aquatic monster mentioned in an old expedition journal.

cards.name: Wellspring Mask Ogerpon ex

attacks.name: Sob
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Torrential Pump
attacks.text: You may shuffle 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your deck. If you do, this attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

cards.name: Shinx

attacks.name: Curiosity
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

cards.flavorText: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. Its fur glows when it's in trouble.

cards.name: Luxio

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: By gathering their tails together, they collectively generate powerful electricity from their claws.

cards.name: Luxray ex

attacks.name: Piercing Gaze
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard a card you find there.

attacks.name: Volt Strike
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Emolga

attacks.name: Sky Wave
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: As Emolga flutters through the air, it crackles with electricity. This Pokémon is cute, but it can cause a lot of trouble.

cards.name: Helioptile

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon's electric moves.

cards.name: Heliolisk

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.

cards.name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Pick and Stick
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

abilities.name: Snack Seek
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.

cards.flavorText: It has a small stomach. If it isn't constantly eating the seeds it keeps in its pockets, it will get hungry immediately.

cards.name: Tadbulb
cards.flavorText: It floats using the electricity stored in its body. When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will float higher off the ground.

cards.name: Bellibolt

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: What appear to be eyeballs are actually organs for discharging the electricity generated by Bellibolt's belly-button dynamo.

cards.name: Wattrel

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon make their nests on coastal cliffs. The nests have a strange, crackling texture, and they're a popular delicacy.

cards.name: Kilowattrel

attacks.name: Wind Power Charge
attacks.text: During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Strong Volt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It uses its throat sac to store electricity generated by its wings. There's hardly any oil in its feathers, so it is a poor swimmer.

cards.name: Iron Thorns ex

attacks.name: Volt Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Initialization
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, Pokémon with a Rule Box in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities, except for Future Pokémon. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Clefairy
cards.flavorText: On nights with a full moon, they gather together and dance. The surrounding area is enveloped in an abnormal magnetic field.

cards.name: Clefable

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

cards.flavorText: It has an acute sense of hearing. It can easily hear a pin being dropped nearly 1,100 yards away.

cards.name: Abra

abilities.name: Teleporter
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may shuffle it and all attached cards into your deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps. The contents of Abra's dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.

cards.name: Kadabra

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps. It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

cards.name: Alakazam

attacks.name: Strange Hacking
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. You may move any number of damage counters from your opponent's Pokémon to their other Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

cards.name: Girafarig

attacks.name: Dual Headbutt
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Girafarig's tail has a small head. It instinctively bites at any foe that approaches the Pokémon from behind.

cards.name: Farigiraf

attacks.name: One-derful Rumble
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Stage 1 Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Eerie Wave
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: The hardened head from the tail protects the head of the main body as Farigiraf whips its long neck around to headbutt enemies.

cards.name: Chimecho

attacks.name: Homeward Chime
attacks.text: Shuffle 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

cards.flavorText: Its cries echo inside its hollow body to emerge as beautiful notes for startling and repelling foes.

cards.name: Flabébé

attacks.name: Bind Wound
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Flabébé rides a red flower. Immediately after birth, this Pokémon begins flying around in search of a flower it likes.

cards.name: Floette

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon uses red wavelengths of light to pour its own energy into flowers and draw forth their latent potential.

cards.name: Florges

abilities.name: Captivating Invitation
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot, and the new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: They say that flower gardens created by Florges are constantly showered with a power that can heal both body and spirit.

cards.name: Swirlix

attacks.name: Sneaky Placement
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

abilities.name: Festival Lead
abilities.text: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn't get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.

cards.name: Slurpuff

attacks.name: Slurp Slurp
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads. If both of them are tails,  your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: By taking in a person's scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It's hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.

cards.name: Sandygast

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

cards.flavorText: It flings sand into the eyes of its prey and tries to close in on them while they're blinded, but since Sandygast is so slow, the prey tends to escape.

cards.name: Palossand

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

cards.flavorText: From the hollows in its arms, it fires the bones of its victims, which are all dried up after being drained of their vitality.

cards.name: Enamorus

attacks.name: Love Resonance
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon in play are the same type as any of your opponent's Pokémon in play, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

cards.name: Scream Tail ex

attacks.name: Scream
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Your opponent can't play any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Munkidori

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

abilities.name: Adrena-Brain
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached, you may move up to 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.

cards.name: Fezandipiti

attacks.name: Energy Feather
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Adrena-Pheromone
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached and is damaged by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

cards.flavorText: Fezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.

cards.name: Sandshrew

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: No matter how high a place it falls from, this Pokémon can save itself by rolling into a ball and bouncing.

cards.name: Sandslash

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: The spikes on its body are made up of its hardened hide. It rolls up and attacks foes with its spikes.

cards.name: Hisuian Growlithe

attacks.name: Blazing Destruction
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

cards.name: Hisuian Arcanine

attacks.name: Proud Fangs
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

cards.name: Nosepass

attacks.name: Power Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: When endangered, it may protect itself by increasing its magnetism and drawing nearby iron objects to its body.

cards.name: Probopass

attacks.name: Assault Laser
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Probopass radiates such a powerful magnetic field that nearby electrical appliances are rendered useless while it's around.

cards.name: Timburr

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle heavy lumber without difficulty.

cards.name: Gurdurr

attacks.name: Superpower
attacks.text: You may do 30 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

cards.name: Conkeldurr

attacks.name: Tantrum
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Gutsy Swing
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, ignore all Energy in this attack's cost.

cards.flavorText: Rather than rely on their strength, they master moves that make good use of centrifugal force to swing around concrete.

cards.name: Greninja ex

attacks.name: Shinobi Blade
attacks.text: You may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Mirage Barrage
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Hawlucha

attacks.name: Prize Count
attacks.text: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape.

cards.name: Glimmet

attacks.name: Rock Shot
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Glimmet's toxic mineral crystals look just like flower petals. This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder like pollen to protect itself.

cards.name: Glimmora

attacks.name: Stun Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Glimmora's petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.

cards.name: Ting-Lu

attacks.name: Ground Crasher
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, and discard that Stadium. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It slowly brings its exceedingly heavy head down upon the ground, splitting the earth open with huge fissures that run over 160 feet deep.

cards.name: Okidogi

abilities.name: Adrena-Power
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached, it gets +100 HP, and the attacks it uses do 100 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Okidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.

cards.name: Cornerstone Mask Ogerpon ex

attacks.name: Demolish
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Cornerstone Stance
abilities.text: Prevent all damage from attacks done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon that have an Ability.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Poochyena

attacks.name: Continuous Steps
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Poochyena pursue their prey in packs. Once the prey is exhausted, the pack finishes it off. But sometimes the prey retaliates, and the pack flees.

cards.name: Mightyena

attacks.name: Hunting Pack
attacks.text: If Mightyena is on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: Once it's caught the scent of faraway prey, the leader gives a signal, and the whole pack moves out.

cards.name: Venipede

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: Venipede and Sizzlipede are similar species, but when the two meet, a huge fight ensues.

cards.name: Whirlipede

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon spins itself rapidly and charges into its opponents. Its top speed is just over 60 mph.

cards.name: Scolipede

attacks.name: Dastardly Jab
attacks.text: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

cards.flavorText: Scolipede latches on to its prey with the claws on its neck before slamming them into the ground and jabbing them with its claws' toxic spikes.

cards.name: Brute Bonnet

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Relentless Punches
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.

cards.name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Big Storm
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

cards.flavorText: People fashion swords from Skarmory's shed feathers, so this Pokémon is a popular element in heraldic designs.

cards.name: Aron

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks— to build up the steel armor that protects its body.

cards.name: Lairon

attacks.name: Steel Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Lairon live in mountains brimming with spring water and iron ore, so these Pokémon often came into conflict with humans in the past.

cards.name: Aggron

attacks.name: Angry Slam
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Guard Claw
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.

cards.name: Heatran

attacks.name: Steel Burst
attacks.text: Discard all Metal Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

abilities.name: Incandescent Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

cards.name: Varoom

attacks.name: Metal Coating
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Metal Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The steel section is Varoom's actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

cards.name: Revavroom

attacks.name: Rally Back
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Running Charge
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Revavroom viciously threatens others with the sound of its exhaust. It sticks its tongue out from its cylindrical mouth and sprays toxic fluids.

cards.name: Applin

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

cards.name: Dipplin

attacks.name: Syrup Catcher
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 70 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

cards.name: Dreepy
cards.flavorText: It has a habit of biting at Clauncher even though it doesn't feed on them. This is said to be vestigial behavior from when Dreepy was alive.

cards.name: Drakloak

abilities.name: Recon Directive
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon stores up energy in its lungs, then shoots it out. It takes care of Dreepy and battles alongside them until they're all grown up.

cards.name: Dragapult ex

attacks.name: Phantom Dive
attacks.text: Put 6 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.name: Tatsugiri

abilities.name: Attract Customers
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal a Supporter card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

cards.flavorText: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

cards.name: Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Mach Cut
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Impromptu Carrier
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

cards.name: Chansey

attacks.name: Lucky Attachment
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: It walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.

cards.name: Blissey ex

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

abilities.name: Happy Switch
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Eevee

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

cards.name: Snorlax

attacks.name: But First, Food
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you do, heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its stomach's digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. Eating things off the ground doesn't bother it at all.

cards.name: Aipom
cards.flavorText: Its tail moves with greater dexterity than its hands. Making deft use of this tail, Aipom lives high among the treetops.

cards.name: Ambipom

abilities.name: Wicked Tail
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may flip 2 coins. For each heads, choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

cards.flavorText: Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things that Ambipom rarely uses its hands.

cards.name: Ducklett

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

cards.flavorText: It strengthens its body by diving into the depths of ponds, swimming around while looking for bog moss to eat.

cards.name: Swanna

attacks.name: Fighting Wings
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It's said that many artists have taken inspiration from the sight of a Swanna performing a beautiful dance in the light of dawn.

cards.name: Bloodmoon Ursaluna ex

attacks.name: Blood Moon
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

abilities.name: Seasoned Skill
abilities.text: Blood Moon used by this Pokémon costs Colorless less for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Accompanying Flute
rules: Reveal the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck. You may choose any number of Basic Pokémon you find there and put those Pokémon onto their Bench. Your opponent shuffles the other cards back into their deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Bug Catching Set
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal up to 2 in any combination of Grass Pokémon and Basic Grass Energy cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Caretaker
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way and if Community Center is in play, shuffle this Caretaker into your deck instead of discarding it.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Carmine
rules: If you go first, you may use this card during your first turn.    Discard your hand and draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Community Center
rules: Once during each player's turn, if they played a Supporter card from their hand this turn, they may heal 10 damage from each of their Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it into the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Cook
rules: Heal 70 damage from your Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Enhanced Hammer
rules: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Festival Grounds
rules: Each Pokémon that has any Energy attached (both yours and your opponent's) recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it into the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Handheld Fan
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), move an Energy from the Attacking Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

cards.name: Hassel
rules: You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.    Look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put up to 3 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Hyper Aroma
rules: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.
Search your deck for up to 3 Stage 1 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Jamming Tower
rules: Pokémon Tools attached to each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) have no effect.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it into the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Kieran
rules: Choose 1:    • Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.  • During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex and Active Pokémon V (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Lana's Aid
rules: Put up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box and Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Love Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon with the same name as 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Lucian
rules: Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player flips a coin. If heads, that player draws 6 cards. If tails, they draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Lucky Helmet
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), draw 2 cards.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

cards.name: Ogre's Mask
rules: Choose a Pokémon ex in your discard pile that has "Ogerpon" in its name, and switch it with 1 of your Pokémon ex in play that has "Ogerpon" in its name. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Perrin
rules: Reveal up to 2 Pokémon in your hand and put them into your deck. If you do, search your deck for up to that many Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Raifort
rules: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and discard any number of them. Put the other cards back in any order.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Scoop Up Cyclone
rules: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.
Put 1 of your Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Secret Box
rules: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

You can use this card only if you discard 3 other cards from your hand.    Search your deck for an Item card, a Pokémon Tool card, a Supporter card, and a Stadium card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Survival Brace
rules: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10. Then, discard this card.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Unfair Stamp
rules: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.    Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 2 cards.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Boomerang Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.    If this card is discarded by an effect of an attack used by the Pokémon this card is attached to, attach this card from your discard pile to that Pokémon after attacking.

cards.name: Legacy Energy
rules: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.    If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card. This effect of your Legacy Energy can't be applied more than once per game.
ACE SPEC: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

cards.name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Slow Crunch
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

cards.name: Sunflora

attacks.name: Redirected Sunlight
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Fire Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Since it converts sunlight into energy, it is always looking in the direction of the sun.

cards.name: Dipplin

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Festival Lead
abilities.text: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

cards.name: Poltchageist

abilities.name: Storehouse Hideaway
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

cards.name: Torkoal

attacks.name: Ramming Shell
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Coal is the source of Torkoal's energy. Large amounts of coal can be found in the mountains where they live.

cards.name: Infernape

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Pyro Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Fire Energy card, a Basic Fighting Energy card, or 1 of each from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

cards.flavorText: Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It is beaten by none in terms of quickness.

cards.name: Froslass

abilities.name: Freezing Shroud
abilities.text: During Pokémon Checkup, put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon that has an Ability (both yours and your opponent's), except any Froslass.

cards.flavorText: It freezes prey by blowing its −58 degree Fahrenheit breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

cards.name: Phione

attacks.name: Beckon
attacks.text: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

cards.name: Cramorant

attacks.name: Spit Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It's an incredibly poor learner because it uses the bare minimum amount of energy for its brain. It focuses on battling instead.

cards.name: Heliolisk

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.

cards.name: Wattrel

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon make their nests on coastal cliffs. The nests have a strange, crackling texture, and they're a popular delicacy.

cards.name: Chimecho

attacks.name: Homeward Chime
attacks.text: Shuffle 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

cards.flavorText: Its cries echo inside its hollow body to emerge as beautiful notes for startling and repelling foes.

cards.name: Enamorus

attacks.name: Love Resonance
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon in play are the same type as any of your opponent's Pokémon in play, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

cards.name: Hisuian Growlithe

attacks.name: Blazing Destruction
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

cards.name: Probopass

attacks.name: Assault Laser
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Probopass radiates such a powerful magnetic field that nearby electrical appliances are rendered useless while it's around.

cards.name: Timburr

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle heavy lumber without difficulty.

cards.name: Lairon

attacks.name: Steel Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Lairon live in mountains brimming with spring water and iron ore, so these Pokémon often came into conflict with humans in the past.

cards.name: Applin

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

cards.name: Tatsugiri

abilities.name: Attract Customers
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal a Supporter card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

cards.flavorText: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

cards.name: Chansey

attacks.name: Lucky Attachment
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: It walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.

cards.name: Eevee

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

cards.name: Sinistcha ex

attacks.name: Re-Brew
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Basic Grass Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those Energy cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Matcha Splash
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Myriad Leaf Shower
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Grass Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw a card.

Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Volt Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Initialization
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, Pokémon with a Rule Box in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities, except for Future Pokémon. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Caretaker
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way and if Community Center is in play, shuffle this Caretaker into your deck instead of discarding it.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Carmine
rules: If you go first, you may use this card during your first turn.    Discard your hand and draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Hassel
rules: You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.    Look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put up to 3 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Kieran
rules: Choose 1:    • Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.  • During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex and Active Pokémon V (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Lana's Aid
rules: Put up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box and Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Lucian
rules: Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player flips a coin. If heads, that player draws 6 cards. If tails, they draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Perrin
rules: Reveal up to 2 Pokémon in your hand and put them into your deck. If you do, search your deck for up to that many Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Buddy-Buddy Poffin

cards.name: Enhanced Hammer
rules: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Rescue Board
rules: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is Colorless less. If that Pokémon's remaining HP is 30 or less, it has no Retreat Cost.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

cards.name: Luminous Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.  If the Pokémon this card is attached to has any other Special Energy attached, this card provides Colorless Energy instead.

attacks.name: Mischievous Tentacles

attacks.name: Colluding Tentacles
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, this attack does 120 damage to the new Active Pokémon. If you didn't play Xerosic's Machinations from your hand during this turn, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Corrosive Winds
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Destructive Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poisonous Musculature

attacks.name: Chain-Crazed
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 130 more damage.

cards.name: Munkidori ex

cards.name: Area Zero Underdepths
rules: Each player who has any Tera Pokémon in play can have up to 8 Pokémon on their Bench.    If a player no longer has any Tera Pokémon in play, that player discards Pokémon from their Bench until they have 5. When this card leaves play, both players discard Pokémon from their Bench until they have 5, and the player who played this card discards first.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Grand Tree
rules: You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for a Stage 1 Pokémon that evolves from 1 of their Basic Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. If that Pokémon was evolved in this way, that player may search their deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, that player shuffles their deck. (Players can't evolve a Basic Pokémon during their first turn or a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.)
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.
