cards.flavorText: If you step on a Geodude sleeping on the ground, you'll hear a zapping sound and feel a tingling shock throughout your entire body.

cards.name: Alolan Graveler
cards.flavorText: Graveler's entire body is electrically charged due to the stones it likes to eat. It is a very quick-tempered Pokémon.

cards.name: Alolan Golem

attacks.name: Electromagnetic Catapult
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Megaton Fall
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It fires rocks charged with electricity. Even if the rock isn't fired that accurately, just grazing an opponent will cause numbness and fainting.

cards.name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Toge Spark
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac.

cards.name: Tapu Koko ex

attacks.name: Linked Lightning
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Alolan Marowak

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon sets the bone it holds on fire and dances through the night as a way to mourn its fallen allies.

cards.name: Oricorio

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: This form of Oricorio has sipped purple nectar. It uses ethereal dance steps to call forth the spirits of the dead.

cards.name: Mimikyu ex

attacks.name: Mischievous Hands
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and put 3 damage counters on each of them.

attacks.name: Ghostly Trip
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Dhelmise

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

cards.name: Impidimp
cards.flavorText: They live in groups, pestering and playing pranks on each other to polish their troublemaking skills.

cards.name: Morgrem
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon absorbs negative emotions and turns them into energy. It's popular with people who tend to think gloomy thoughts.

cards.name: Grimmsnarl

attacks.name: Shadowy Knot
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

cards.flavorText: It lives deep within the forest. Even after evolving into this splendid form, it hasn't given up on its petty misdeeds and pranks.

cards.name: Milcery
cards.flavorText: When attacked, it tackles its foe and covers them in cream. With its foe either blinded or astonished by deliciousness, Milcery flees.

cards.name: Alcremie ex

abilities.name: Confectionary Gift
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Cubone
cards.flavorText: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

cards.name: Toedscool
cards.flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

cards.name: Toedscruel

abilities.name: Secret Forest Path
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is ColorlessColorless less.

cards.flavorText: It coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.

cards.name: Paldean Wooper

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It's dangerous for Wooper to travel alone. They line up in groups of three or four and help each other as they walk around the wetlands.

cards.name: Paldean Clodsire ex

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Lokix
cards.flavorText: It uses its normally folded third set of legs when in Showdown Mode. This places a huge burden on its body, so it can't stay in this mode for long.

cards.name: Bombirdier

attacks.name: Drop Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

cards.name: Galarian Stunfisk

attacks.name: Bounding Bite
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.

cards.name: Cufant
cards.flavorText: Cufant can lift loads weighing five tons. In the mornings, it heads into caves with its herd, in search of the ore on which these Pokémon feed.

cards.name: Copperajah
cards.flavorText: Copperajah are prideful, cantankerous Pokémon. Specimens with vibrant green skin command the respect of others of their kind.

cards.name: Kecleon

attacks.name: Stealth Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It changes its hue to blend into its surroundings. If no one takes notice of it for too long, it will pout and never reveal itself.

cards.name: Tropius

attacks.name: Fruit Bearing
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

cards.flavorText: Delicious fruits grew out from around its neck because it always ate the same kind of fruit.

cards.name: Audino

attacks.name: Beckon
attacks.text: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

cards.flavorText: Audino's sense of hearing is superb. Not even a pebble rolling along over a mile away will escape Audino's ears.

cards.name: Minccino
cards.flavorText: These Pokémon like things neat and tidy. They are always sweeping and dusting their habitat, using their tails as brooms.

cards.name: Cinccino
cards.flavorText: The oil that seeps from its body is really smooth. For people troubled by bad skin, this oil is an effective treatment.

cards.name: Drampa
cards.flavorText: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

cards.name: Skwovet

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

cards.name: Greedent

attacks.name: Gluttonous Tail
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon makes off with heaps of fallen berries by wrapping them in its tail, which is roughly twice the length of its body.

cards.name: Cramorant

attacks.name: Ceaseless Spitting
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It's an incredibly poor learner because it uses the bare minimum amount of energy for its brain. It focuses on battling instead.

cards.name: Lechonk
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon spurns all but the finest of foods. Its body gives off an herblike scent that bug Pokémon detest.

cards.name: Oinkologne

attacks.name: Aromatic Stomps
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: This is a meticulous Pokémon that likes to keep things tidy. It shrouds itself in a floral aroma that soothes the Pokémon around it.

cards.name: Squawkabilly

attacks.name: Add On
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

cards.flavorText: Green-feathered flocks hold the most sway. When they're out searching for food in the mornings and evenings, it gets very noisy.

cards.name: Billy & O'Nare
rules: Draw 2 cards. Then, if you have 10 or more cards in your hand, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Black Belt's Training
rules: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 40 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Black Belt's Training
rules: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 40 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Black Belt's Training
rules: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 40 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Rellor

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: It rolls its mud ball around while the energy it needs for evolution matures. Eventually the time comes for it to evolve.

cards.name: Rabsca ex

attacks.name: Upside-Down Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards from the bottom of your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 90 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Hippopotas

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It shrouds itself in sand to ward off germs. It travels easily through the sands of the desert.

cards.name: Hippowdon

attacks.name: Super Sandstorm
attacks.text: This attack also does 40 damage to each Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of fearsome strength. It raises vast quantities of sand while attacking.

cards.name: TM Machine
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards that have "Technical Machine" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Jamming Tower
rules: Pokémon Tools attached to each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) have no effect.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Antique Cover Fossil

abilities.name: Protective Cover
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

rules: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.  At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
cards.flavorText: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

cards.name: Buddy-Buddy Poffin
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Night Stretcher
rules: Put a Pokémon or a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Paldean Wooper

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It's dangerous for Wooper to travel alone. They line up in groups of three or four and help each other as they walk around the wetlands.

cards.name: Meowth

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny object, the gold coin on its head shines, too.

cards.name: Grubbin

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It spits sticky threads and winds them around branches, then swings nimbly from tree to tree in a pendulum-like motion.

cards.name: Budew

attacks.name: Itchy Pollen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

cards.flavorText: The pollen it releases contains poison. If this Pokémon is raised on clean water, the poison’s toxicity is increased.

cards.name: Salandit

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Only female Salandit can produce gas laden with pheromones. Males entranced by this gas will do whatever the females tell them.

cards.name: Scorbunny

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Fire energy gathers in the pads of its feet, raising their temperature. Once hot, Scorbunny’s footpads can deal heavy damage to opponents.

cards.name: Salazzle

attacks.name: Sudden Scorching
attacks.text: Your opponent discards a card from their hand. If this Pokémon evolved from Salandit during this turn, your opponent discards 2 more cards.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When two Salazzle meet, they will use their pheromone gas to fight over the males in each other’s group.

cards.name: Pikachu ex

attacks.name: Topaz Bolt
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Resolute Heart
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Clefable

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

cards.flavorText: It has an acute sense of hearing. It can easily hear a pin being dropped nearly 1,100 yards away.

cards.name: Togekiss

abilities.name: Wonder Kiss
abilities.text: When your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out, flip a coin. If heads, take 1 more Prize card. The effect of Wonder Kiss doesn't stack.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.

cards.name: Poochyena

attacks.name: Gnaw Off
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

cards.name: Mightyena

attacks.name: Kick Away
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

cards.name: Dreepy
cards.flavorText: It has a habit of biting at Clauncher even though it doesn’t feed on them. This is said to be vestigial behavior from when Dreepy was alive.

cards.name: Dragapult ex

attacks.name: Phantom Dive
attacks.text: Put 6 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Cinderace ex

attacks.name: Flare Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Flare Strike.

attacks.name: Garnet Volley
attacks.text: This attack does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Sneasel

attacks.name: Beset
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: This is a smart and sneaky Pokémon. A pair may work together to steal eggs by having one lure the parents away.

cards.name: Togepi
cards.flavorText: The shell seems to be filled with joy. It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.

cards.name: Drakloak

abilities.name: Recon Directive
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon stores up energy in its lungs, then shoots it out. It takes care of Dreepy and battles alongside them until they’re all grown up.

cards.name: Counter Gain
rules: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, attacks used by the Pokémon this card is attached to cost Colorless less.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Charjabug
cards.flavorText: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

cards.name: Vikavolt

attacks.name: Volt Switch
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Lightning Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When carrying a Charjabug, Vikavolt can receive electricity from it and then rapidly fire powerful electromagnetic beams from its large jaws.

cards.name: Clefairy
cards.flavorText: On nights with a full moon, they gather together and dance. The surrounding area is enveloped in an abnormal magnetic field.

cards.name: Munkidori

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

abilities.name: Adrena-Brain
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached, you may move up to 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.

cards.name: Terapagos

attacks.name: Prism Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Tera Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: The shell is made of crystallized Terastal energy. When struck by a move, this shell absorbs the move’s energy and transfers it to Terapagos.

cards.name: Budew

attacks.name: Itchy Pollen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

cards.flavorText: The pollen it releases contains poison. If this Pokémon is raised on clean water, the poison’s toxicity is increased.

cards.name: Raboot
cards.flavorText: While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves, it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its flames have heated up its forehead.

cards.name: Weavile

attacks.name: Hail Claw
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: They travel in groups of four or five, leaving signs for one another on trees and rocks. They bring down their prey with coordinated attacks.

cards.name: Togetic

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

cards.name: Fezandipiti ex

attacks.name: Cruel Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Flip the Script
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, you may draw 3 cards. You can't use more than 1 Flip the Script Ability each turn.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Fan Rotom

attacks.name: Assault Landing
attacks.text: If there is no Stadium in play, this attack does nothing.

abilities.name: Fan Call
abilities.text: Once during your first turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 Colorless Pokémon with 100 HP or less, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can't use more than 1 Fan Call Ability during your turn.

cards.flavorText: There are several appliances that Rotom can inspirit, but the first one to be developed was the electric fan.

cards.name: Terapagos ex

attacks.name: Unified Beatdown
attacks.text: If you go second, you can't use this attack during your first turn. This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crown Opal
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic non-Colorless Pokémon.

rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Buddy-Buddy Poffin
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Glass Trumpet
rules: You can use this card only if you have any Tera Pokémon in play.  Choose up to 2 of your Benched Colorless Pokémon and attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of them.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Night Stretcher
rules: Put a Pokémon or a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Surfer
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Surfer

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: N's Darmanitan

attacks.name: Back Draft
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Basic Energy card in your opponent's discard pile.

attacks.name: Flamebody Cannon
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack also does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon's power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

cards.name: Iono's Kilowattrel

abilities.name: Flashing Draw
abilities.text: You must discard a Basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

cards.flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

cards.name: Lillie's Ribombee

abilities.name: Inviting Wink
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand and you put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto their Bench.

cards.flavorText: Ribombee can sense when people or Pokémon are feeling down, and it will cheer them up with handmade pollen puffs.

cards.name: Hop's Snorlax

attacks.name: Dynamic Press
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 80 damage to itself.

abilities.name: Extra Helpings
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Hop's Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). The effect of Extra Helpings doesn't stack.

cards.flavorText: This gluttonous Pokémon eats constantly, apart from when it's asleep. It devours nearly 900 pounds of food per day.

cards.name: N's Zorua
cards.flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

cards.name: Hop's Zacian ex

attacks.name: Insta-Strike
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Brave Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Brave Slash.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Iono's Bellibolt ex

attacks.name: Thunderous Bolt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

abilities.name: Electric Streamer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Iono's Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Lillie's Clefairy ex

attacks.name: Full Moon Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

abilities.name: Fairy Zone
abilities.text: The Weakness of each of your opponent's Dragon Pokémon in play is now Psychic. (Apply Weakness as ×2.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Wobbuffet

attacks.name: Rocket Mirror
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Team Rocket's Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

cards.name: Marnie's Morpeko

attacks.name: Spiky Wheel
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Morpeko eats seeds constantly to keep its hunger at bay. If it's hungry for too long, it grows wild and brutish.

cards.name: Steven's Beldum
cards.flavorText: The magnetic force generated by its body repels the ground's natural magnetism, letting it float.

cards.name: Caterpie
cards.flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

cards.name: Metapod

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 60 or less.

cards.flavorText: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can't withstand a harsh attack.

cards.name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Scale Hurricane
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.

cards.name: Paras

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.

cards.name: Parasect

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Scissor Swing
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.

cards.name: Maractus

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

abilities.name: Exploding Needles
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They're jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert.

cards.name: Karrablast

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It spits a liquid from its mouth to melt through Shelmet's shell. Karrablast doesn't eat the shell— it eats only the contents.

cards.name: Shelmet

attacks.name: Shell Smack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

cards.flavorText: It has a strange physiology that responds to electricity. When together with Karrablast, Shelmet evolves for some reason.

cards.name: Accelgor

attacks.name: Poisonous Ploy
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Discarding its shell made it nimble. To keep itself from dehydrating, it wraps its body in bands of membrane.

cards.name: Sprigatito

attacks.name: Tons of Treading
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.

cards.name: Floragato

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage, and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

cards.name: Meowscarada

attacks.name: Rising Bloom
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 90 more damage.

abilities.name: Showtime
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

cards.name: Magmar

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: Found near the mouth of a volcano. This fire-breather's body temperature is nearly 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit.

cards.name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

abilities.name: Magma Surge
abilities.text: During Pokémon Checkup, put 3 more damage counters on your opponent's Burned Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When shooting 3,600 degree Fahrenheit fireballs from its arms, its body takes on a whitish hue from the intense heat.

cards.name: N's Darumaka
cards.flavorText: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it's pushed or pulled.

cards.name: N's Darmanitan

attacks.name: Back Draft
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Basic Energy card in your opponent's discard pile.

attacks.name: Flamebody Cannon
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack also does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon's power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

cards.name: Volcanion ex

attacks.name: Scorching Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Scalding Steam
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Articuno

attacks.name: Frigid Fluttering
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can control ice at will. Articuno is said to live in snowy mountains riddled with permafrost.

cards.name: Remoraid

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine's underside to scavenge for leftovers.

cards.name: Octillery

attacks.name: Aqua Wash
attacks.text: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

attacks.name: Octo Beatdown
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

cards.name: Lotad
cards.flavorText: The leaf on its head is naturally dirt repellent and will stay clean even after transporting Pokémon that are covered in mud.

cards.name: Lombre

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Old folklore in Kitakami tells of a mischievous child who was reborn as a Pokémon.

cards.name: Ludicolo

abilities.name: Vibrant Dance
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon in play get +40 HP. The effect of Vibrant Dance doesn't stack.

cards.flavorText: There are structures throughout its whole body that produce energy when hit by sound waves with a cheerful rhythm.

cards.name: Wailmer
cards.flavorText: When it sucks in a large volume of seawater, it becomes like a big, bouncy ball. It eats a ton of food daily.

cards.name: Wailord

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its immense size is the reason for its popularity. Wailord watching is a favorite sightseeing activity in various parts of the world.

cards.name: Iono's Voltorb

attacks.name: Voltaic Chain
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to all of your Iono's Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

cards.name: Iono's Electrode

attacks.name: Thump-Thump Boom
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 100 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

cards.flavorText: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

cards.name: N's Joltik

attacks.name: Zapping Short
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you discarded a Pokémon Tool in this way, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Since it can't generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.

cards.name: Iono's Tadbulb
cards.flavorText: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

cards.name: Iono's Bellibolt ex

attacks.name: Thunderous Bolt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

abilities.name: Electric Streamer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Iono's Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Iono's Wattrel

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

cards.name: Iono's Kilowattrel

abilities.name: Flashing Draw
abilities.text: You must discard a Basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

cards.flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

cards.name: Lillie's Clefairy ex

attacks.name: Full Moon Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

abilities.name: Fairy Zone
abilities.text: The Weakness of each of your opponent's Dragon Pokémon in play is now Psychic. (Apply Weakness as ×2.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Mr. Mime

attacks.name: Mimic
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It's known for its top-notch pantomime skills. It protects itself from all sorts of attacks by emitting auras from its fingers to create walls.

cards.name: Shuppet
cards.flavorText: It loves to feed on feelings like envy and malice. Its upright horn catches the emotions of people.

cards.name: Banette

attacks.name: Cursed Words
attacks.text: Your opponent chooses 3 cards from their hand and shuffles those cards into their deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.

cards.name: Beldum
cards.flavorText: The magnetic force generated by its body repels the ground's natural magnetism, letting it float.

cards.name: Metang
cards.flavorText: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its two brains are linked, amplifying its psychic power.

cards.name: Metagross

attacks.name: Conjoined Beams
attacks.text: If Beldum and Metang are on your Bench, this attack does 150 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It folds its four legs when flying. Its four brains are said to be superior to a supercomputer.

cards.name: N's Sigilyph

attacks.name: Victory Symbol
attacks.text: If you use this attack when you have exactly 1 Prize card remaining, you win this game.

cards.flavorText: A discovery was made in the desert where Sigilyph fly. The ruins of what may have been an ancient city were found beneath the sands.

cards.name: Lillie's Cutiefly

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When it senses the auras of people or Pokémon who are having fun, it will draw close and give them a painful poke with its long proboscis.

cards.name: Lillie's Ribombee

abilities.name: Inviting Wink
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand and you put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto their Bench.

cards.flavorText: Ribombee can sense when people or Pokémon are feeling down, and it will cheer them up with handmade pollen puffs.

cards.name: Lillie's Comfey

attacks.name: Inviting Flowers
attacks.text: You may search your deck for any number of Basic Lillie's Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Fade Out
attacks.text: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

cards.flavorText: It uses its vine to pick flowers. The flowers that Comfey attaches to itself develop healing properties.

cards.name: Swinub

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It searches for food by digging into the ground with its snout. Even frozen ground doesn't give it any trouble.

cards.name: Piloswine
cards.flavorText: Covered by a shaggy coat, it is resistant to the cold. Its tusks of ice thicken when it snows.

cards.name: Mamoswine ex

attacks.name: Rumbling March
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Stage 2 Pokémon on your Bench.

abilities.name: Mammoth Hauler
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

cards.name: Pupitar

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This pupa flies around wildly by venting with great force the gas pressurized inside its body.

cards.name: Regirock

attacks.name: Breaching Lariat
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

abilities.name: Rock Armor
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Cutting-edge technology was used to study the internals of this Pokémon's rock body, but nothing was found—not even a brain or a heart.

cards.name: Pancham

attacks.name: Focus Fist
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Wanting to make sure it's taken seriously, Pancham's always giving others a glare. But if it's not focusing, it ends up smiling.

cards.name: Rockruff

attacks.name: Dig It Up
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is very friendly when it's young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

cards.name: Lycanroc

attacks.name: Clamping Fangs
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Spike-Clad
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Spiky Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Lycanroc attacks its prey with its sharp claws and fangs. It loyally obeys the instructions of a Trainer it trusts.

cards.name: Hop's Silicobra

attacks.name: Turf Maker
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Silicobra's neck pouch, which can inflate and deflate like a balloon, gets more elastic each time Silicobra sheds its skin.

cards.name: Hop's Sandaconda

attacks.name: Rumble
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Break Ground
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It will expand its body as much as it can and then contract itself, blasting out sand with enough force to wash away a dump truck.

cards.name: Klawf

attacks.name: Snipping Pincers
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can't remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

cards.name: Koffing
cards.flavorText: Toxic gas is held within its thin, balloon-shaped body, so it can cause massive explosions.

cards.name: Weezing

attacks.name: Pervasive Gas
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Crazy Blast
attacks.text: If this Pokémon used Pervasive Gas during your last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Top-grade perfume is made using its internal poison gases by diluting them to the highest level.

cards.name: Tyranitar

attacks.name: Cracking Stomp
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

abilities.name: Daunting Gaze
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand.

cards.flavorText: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn't care about others.

cards.name: N's Purrloin

attacks.name: Thieving Swipe
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

cards.flavorText: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

cards.name: N's Zorua
cards.flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

cards.name: N's Zoroark ex

attacks.name: Night Joker
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Benched N's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

abilities.name: Trade
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Pangoro

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

attacks.name: Power Tackle
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.

cards.name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Iron Buster
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: It charges its enemies, lances at the ready. An image of one of its duels is captured in a famous painting of Escavalier clashing with Sirfetch'd.

cards.name: N's Klink

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.

cards.name: N's Klang

attacks.name: Spinning Gears
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon's rotation speed increases sharply.

cards.name: N's Klinklang

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.

cards.name: Magearna

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

abilities.name: Auto Heal
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, heal 90 damage from that Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Built roughly 500 years ago by a scientist, the part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.

cards.name: Hop's Corviknight

attacks.name: Shoot Through
attacks.text: This attack also does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Although its wings have partly turned to steel and become heavy as a result, this Pokémon flies through the skies with ease.

cards.name: Hop's Zacian ex

attacks.name: Insta-Strike
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Brave Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Brave Slash.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Bagon

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Its steel-hard head can shatter boulders. It longingly hopes for wings to grow so it can fly.

cards.name: Shelgon

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: The cells within its shell transform with explosive speed, preparing it for evolution.

cards.name: Salamence ex

attacks.name: Wide Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dragon Impact
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Dragon's Fury
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Dragon Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.

cards.name: N's Reshiram

attacks.name: Powerful Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down.

cards.name: Hop's Snorlax

attacks.name: Dynamic Press
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 80 damage to itself.

abilities.name: Extra Helpings
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Hop's Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). The effect of Extra Helpings doesn't stack.

cards.flavorText: This gluttonous Pokémon eats constantly, apart from when it's asleep. It devours nearly 900 pounds of food per day.

cards.name: Sentret
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is extremely cautious. Its supple tail is well muscled and firm to the touch.

cards.name: Furret
cards.flavorText: It raises its offspring inside a long, narrow nest. Once they're old enough, it takes them outside the nest to prepare them for independence.

cards.name: Dunsparce

attacks.name: Trading Places
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It creates mazes in dark locations. When spotted, it flees into the ground by digging with its tail.

cards.name: Dudunsparce ex

attacks.name: Tenacious Tail
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon ex in play.

attacks.name: Destructive Drill
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Noibat

attacks.name: Rapid Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from its large ears to search for fruit to eat. It mistakes Applin for its food.

cards.name: Noivern

attacks.name: Frightening Howl
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

abilities.name: Tuning Echo
abilities.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, ignore all Energy in the cost of Frightening Howl used by this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Ultrasonic waves emitted by a Noivern can pulverize a large boulder. This Pokémon has a cruel disposition.

cards.name: Komala

attacks.name: Slumbering Smack
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep. During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 100 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Komala spends its entire life sleeping. It feeds on leaves that contain a potent poison only Komala can break down.

cards.name: Hop's Rookidee

attacks.name: Intimidating Stare
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: The females are fussier than the males. If another creature dirties a female Rookidee's wings, it'll peck the offender relentlessly in a burning rage.

cards.name: Hop's Corvisquire
cards.flavorText: This intelligent Pokémon will quickly learn how to use any tool it can hold in its beak or its talons.

cards.name: Hop's Wooloo
cards.flavorText: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

cards.name: Hop's Dubwool

abilities.name: Defiant Horn
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

cards.flavorText: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

cards.name: Hop's Cramorant

attacks.name: Fickle Spitting
attacks.text: If your opponent doesn't have exactly 3 or 4 Prize cards remaining, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Cramorant instinctively swallow everything whole. They're at their most formidable when they're struggling to swallow overly large prey.

cards.name: Brock's Scouting
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon or 1 Evolution Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Hop's Bag
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Hop's Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Hop's Choice Band
rules: Attacks used by the Hop's Pokémon this card is attached to cost Colorless less and do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Iris's Fighting Spirit
rules: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.  Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Levincia
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may put up to 2 Basic Lightning Energy cards from their discard pile into their hand.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Lillie's Pearl
rules: If the Lillie's Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: N's Castle
rules: N's Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Retreat Cost.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: N's PP Up
rules: Attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched N's Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Postwick
rules: Attacks used by Hop's Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) do 30 more damage to the opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Redeemable Ticket
rules: Count your Prize cards, shuffle them, and put them on the bottom of your deck. Then, take that many cards from the top of your deck and put them face down as your Prize cards.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Ruffian
rules: Discard a Pokémon Tool and a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Super Potion
rules: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, discard an Energy from that Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Spiky Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

cards.name: Maractus

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

abilities.name: Exploding Needles
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They're jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert.

cards.name: Articuno

attacks.name: Frigid Fluttering
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can control ice at will. Articuno is said to live in snowy mountains riddled with permafrost.

cards.name: Wailord

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its immense size is the reason for its popularity. Wailord watching is a favorite sightseeing activity in various parts of the world.

cards.name: Iono's Kilowattrel

abilities.name: Flashing Draw
abilities.text: You must discard a Basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

cards.flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

cards.name: Lillie's Ribombee

abilities.name: Inviting Wink
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand and you put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto their Bench.

cards.flavorText: Ribombee can sense when people or Pokémon are feeling down, and it will cheer them up with handmade pollen puffs.

cards.name: Swinub

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It searches for food by digging into the ground with its snout. Even frozen ground doesn't give it any trouble.

cards.name: Lycanroc

attacks.name: Clamping Fangs
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Spike-Clad
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Spiky Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Lycanroc attacks its prey with its sharp claws and fangs. It loyally obeys the instructions of a Trainer it trusts.

cards.name: N's Reshiram

attacks.name: Powerful Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down.

cards.name: Furret
cards.flavorText: It raises its offspring inside a long, narrow nest. Once they're old enough, it takes them outside the nest to prepare them for independence.

cards.name: Noibat

attacks.name: Rapid Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from its large ears to search for fruit to eat. It mistakes Applin for its food.

cards.name: Hop's Wooloo
cards.flavorText: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

cards.name: Volcanion ex

attacks.name: Scorching Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Scalding Steam
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Iono's Bellibolt ex

attacks.name: Thunderous Bolt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

abilities.name: Electric Streamer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Iono's Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Lillie's Clefairy ex

attacks.name: Full Moon Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

abilities.name: Fairy Zone
abilities.text: The Weakness of each of your opponent's Dragon Pokémon in play is now Psychic. (Apply Weakness as ×2.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Mamoswine ex

attacks.name: Rumbling March
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Stage 2 Pokémon on your Bench.

abilities.name: Mammoth Hauler
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: N's Zoroark ex

attacks.name: Night Joker
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Benched N's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

abilities.name: Trade
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Hop's Zacian ex

attacks.name: Insta-Strike
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Brave Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Brave Slash.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Salamence ex

attacks.name: Wide Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dragon Impact
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Dudunsparce ex

attacks.name: Tenacious Tail
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon ex in play.

attacks.name: Destructive Drill
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Brock's Scouting
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon or 1 Evolution Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

rules: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.  Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Ruffian
rules: Discard a Pokémon Tool and a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Scorching Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned.

attacks.name: Thunderous Bolt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Iono's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Full Moon Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Benched N's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.
