cards.name: Hop's Zacian ex

attacks.name: Insta-Strike
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Brave Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Brave Slash.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Salamence ex

attacks.name: Wide Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dragon Impact
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Iono's Bellibolt ex

attacks.name: Thunderous Bolt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

abilities.name: Electric Streamer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Iono's Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: N's Zoroark ex

attacks.name: Night Joker
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Benched N's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

abilities.name: Trade
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Spiky Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

cards.name: Ethan's Pinsir

attacks.name: Rallying Horn
attacks.text: If any of your Ethan's Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon clamps its pincers down on its prey and then either splits the prey in half or flings it away.

cards.name: Yanma

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: As Yanma surveys its territory, it periodically stops to hover in place by flapping its wings at high speeds.

cards.name: Yanmega ex

attacks.name: Jet Cyclone
attacks.text: Move 3 Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Buzzing Boost
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may search your deck for up to 3 Basic Grass Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Pineco
cards.flavorText: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

cards.name: Shroomish
cards.flavorText: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

cards.name: Breloom

attacks.name: Damage Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

cards.name: Cynthia's Roselia
cards.flavorText: It uses the different poisons in each hand separately when it attacks. The stronger its aroma, the healthier it is.

cards.name: Cynthia's Roserade

abilities.name: Cheer On to Glory
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Cynthia's Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: The poison in its right hand is quick acting. The poison in its left hand is slow acting. Both are life threatening.

cards.name: Mow Rotom

attacks.name: Trimming Mower
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The lawn mower is one of the household appliances that led to the development of the Rotom Dex.

cards.name: Shaymin

abilities.name: Flower Curtain
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

cards.flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

cards.name: Dwebble

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.

cards.name: Crustle

attacks.name: Superb Scissors
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Mysterious Rock Inn
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.

cards.flavorText: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They're mighty enough to crack Rhyperior's carapace.

cards.name: Fomantis
cards.flavorText: Fomantis hates having its naps interrupted. It fires off beams using energy it gathers by bathing in the sun.

cards.name: Lurantis

attacks.name: Petal Blade Dance
attacks.text: Discard 2 Basic Grass Energy cards from your hand. If you can't discard 2 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: By masquerading as a bug Pokémon, it lowers the guard of actual bug Pokémon lured in by a scent of sweet flowers. Its sickles bring them down.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Blipbug

attacks.name: Searching Eyes
attacks.text: Look at 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards.

cards.flavorText: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

cards.name: Applin

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It grows by eating the flesh of an apple. Applin uses its own body fluid to strengthen the apple's skin and prevent it from rotting.

cards.name: Dipplin

attacks.name: Energy Loop
attacks.text: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

cards.flavorText: Dipplin is two creatures in one Pokémon. Its evolution was triggered by a special apple grown only in one place.

cards.name: Hydrapple

attacks.name: Hydra Breath
attacks.text: Discard 6 Basic Grass Energy cards from your hand, and Knock Out your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you can't discard 6 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Seven syrpents live inside an apple made of syrup. The syrpent in the center is the commander.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Tarountula

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon's natural enemy.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Spidops

attacks.name: Rocket Rush
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

abilities.name: Charging Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

cards.name: Smoliv
cards.flavorText: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

cards.name: Dolliv

attacks.name: Nutrients
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

cards.name: Arboliva ex

attacks.name: Oil Salvo
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon 6 times. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) For each time you chose a Pokémon, do 20 damage to it. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Aroma Shot
attacks.text: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Teal Mask Ogerpon

attacks.name: Grass Kagura
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Ogre's Hammer
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Ogre's Hammer.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.

cards.name: Growlithe
cards.flavorText: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

cards.name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Punishing Fang
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Darkness Pokémon, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.flavorText: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

cards.name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: If you've been accepted by Ponyta, its burning mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.

cards.name: Rapidash

abilities.name: Hurried Gait
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

cards.flavorText: The fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd's pace and direction of travel.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Moltres ex

attacks.name: Flame Screen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Evil Incineration
attacks.text: Discard a Team Rocket's Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard your opponent's Active Pokémon and all attached cards.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Ethan's Cyndaquil

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It usually stays hunched over. If it is angry or surprised, it shoots flames out of its back.

cards.name: Ethan's Quilava

abilities.name: Bonded by the Journey
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for an Ethan's Adventure card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Before battle, it turns its back on its opponent to demonstrate how ferociously its fire blazes.

cards.name: Ethan's Typhlosion

attacks.name: Buddy Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each Ethan's Adventure card in your discard pile.

cards.flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur to cause huge explosions.

cards.name: Ethan's Slugma
cards.flavorText: Its lava body can cool and chip away at times, but a magma bath will heal it right up.

cards.name: Ethan's Magcargo

attacks.name: Lava Burst
attacks.text: Discard up to 5 Fire Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

abilities.name: Melt Away
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon lives near the craters of volcanoes. It stores fire energy in its shell of cooled and hardened magma.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Houndour
cards.flavorText: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Houndoom

attacks.name: Cruel Coal
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

cards.name: Ethan's Ho-Oh ex

attacks.name: Shining Feathers
attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from each of your Pokémon.

abilities.name: Golden Flame
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic Fire Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Ethan's Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Torchic

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: Torchic feels toasty warm if you hug it. It has a flame sac inside its belly, and the flames burn continuously as long as Torchic has life in it.

cards.name: Combusken

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It boosts its concentration by emitting harsh cries. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

cards.name: Blaziken

attacks.name: Inferno Kick Flurry
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

cards.name: Heat Rotom

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: If the convection microwave oven is not working properly, then the Rotom inhabiting it will become lethargic.

cards.name: Hearthflame Mask Ogerpon

attacks.name: Fire Kagura
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: This form is the most aggressive, bombarding enemies with the intensity of flames blazing within a hearth.

cards.name: Misty's Psyduck

abilities.name: Flustered Leap
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may discard the bottom card of your deck. If you do, discard all cards from this Pokémon and put this Pokémon on top of your deck.

cards.flavorText: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

cards.name: Misty's Staryu

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Fish Pokémon nibble at it, but Staryu isn't bothered. Its body regenerates quickly, even if part of it is completely torn off.

cards.name: Misty's Starmie

attacks.name: Abrupt Flash
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Misty's Staryu during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Starmie swims by spinning its body at high speed. As this Pokémon cruises through the ocean, it absorbs tiny plankton.

cards.name: Misty's Magikarp

abilities.name: So Submerged
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

cards.name: Misty's Gyarados

attacks.name: Splashing Panic
attacks.text: Discard the top 7 cards of your deck, and this attack does 70 damage for each Misty's Pokémon that you discarded in this way.

cards.flavorText: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

cards.name: Misty's Lapras

attacks.name: Swim Together
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Misty's Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It ferries people across the sea on its back. It may sing an enchanting cry if it is in a good mood.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Articuno

attacks.name: Dark Frost
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Team Rocket's Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

abilities.name: Repelling Veil
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to your Basic Team Rocket's Pokémon. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can control ice at will. Articuno is said to live in snowy mountains riddled with permafrost.

cards.name: Cynthia's Feebas

attacks.name: Undulate
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This tough Pokémon is perfectly fine even in dirty water. However, due to its ragged, shabby appearance, it isn't popular.

cards.name: Cynthia's Milotic

attacks.name: Aqua Split
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Its lovely scales are described as rainbow colored. They change color depending on the viewing angle.

cards.name: Clamperl

attacks.name: Shell Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Clamperl's pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder's pearls.

cards.name: Huntail

abilities.name: Diver's Catch
abilities.text: When 1 of your Water Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, you may put all Basic Water Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand instead of the discard pile.

cards.flavorText: Deep seas are their habitat. According to tradition, when Huntail wash up onshore, something unfortunate will happen.

cards.name: Gorebyss

attacks.name: Crescendo Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. Before doing damage, you may attach any number of Basic Water Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It sucks bodily fluids out of its prey. The leftover meat sinks to the seafloor, where it becomes food for other Pokémon.

cards.name: Buizel
cards.flavorText: It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

cards.name: Floatzel

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

cards.name: Snover
cards.flavorText: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain's lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

cards.name: Abomasnow

attacks.name: Frozen Wood
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has 2 or more Grass Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

cards.name: Wash Rotom

attacks.name: Manual Wash
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Rotom has entered a washing machine. It nods with satisfaction after it floods the surrounding area.

cards.name: Arrokuda

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Arrokuda can swim quickly only in a straight line. The ones that become sluggish from overeating are the first to be targeted by flocks of Wattrel.

cards.name: Barraskewda

attacks.name: Dive
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It spins its tail fins to leap from the water, then viciously bites down on Wingull flying close to the water's surface.

cards.name: Cetoddle
cards.flavorText: This species left the ocean and began living on land a very long time ago. It seems to be closely related to Wailmer.

cards.name: Cetitan ex

attacks.name: Crushing Press
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play. If you do, this attack does 140 more damage.

abilities.name: Snow Camouflage
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Dondozo ex

attacks.name: Avenging Billow
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamic Dive
attacks.text: You may do 120 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Wellspring Mask Ogerpon

attacks.name: Water Kagura
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Water Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This form excels in both attack and defense. It ceaselessly unleashes moves like a spring gushes water.

cards.name: Electabuzz
cards.flavorText: Its body constantly discharges electricity. Getting close to it will make your hair stand on end.

cards.name: Electivire ex

attacks.name: Dual Bolt
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: High-Voltage Press
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Zapdos

attacks.name: Jamming Wing
attacks.text: You may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wicked Thunder
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Team Rocket's Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Zapdos is a legendary bird Pokémon. It's said that when Zapdos rubs its feathers together, lightning will fall immediately after.

cards.name: Ethan's Pichu

attacks.name: Zapping Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: It is unskilled at storing electric power. Any kind of shock causes it to discharge energy spontaneously.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Mareep

attacks.name: Procurement
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: If static electricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Flaaffy

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won't grow.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Ampharos

abilities.name: Darkest Impulse
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent plays a Pokémon from their hand to evolve 1 of their Pokémon, put 4 damage counters on that Pokémon. The effect of Darkest Impulse doesn't stack.

cards.flavorText: The bright light on its tail can be seen far away. It has been treasured since ancient times as a beacon.

cards.name: Electrike
cards.flavorText: It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.

cards.name: Manectric

attacks.name: Flash Impact
attacks.text: This attack also does 40 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It rarely appears before people. It is said to nest where lightning has fallen.

cards.name: Rotom

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

cards.name: Zeraora

attacks.name: Thunder Raid
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 210 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon ex. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Electricity sparks from the pads on its limbs. Wherever Zeraora runs, lightning flashes and thunder echoes.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Drowzee

attacks.name: Hypnotic Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

cards.flavorText: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children's are much tastier.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Hypno

attacks.name: Bench Manipulation
attacks.text: Your opponent flips a coin for each of their Benched Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each tails. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

cards.flavorText: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves, such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Mewtwo ex

attacks.name: Erasure Ball
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

abilities.name: Power Saver
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't attack unless you have 4 or more Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Wobbuffet

attacks.name: Rocket Mirror
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Team Rocket's Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

cards.name: Steven's Baltoy

attacks.name: Summoning Sign
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Steven's Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

cards.name: Steven's Claydol

attacks.name: Eerie Light
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Clay Blast
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Chingling

attacks.name: Chiming Commotion
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: There is an orb inside its mouth. When it hops, the orb bounces all over and makes a ringing sound.

cards.name: Steven's Carbink

abilities.name: Stone Palace
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, all of your Steven's Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). The effect of Stone Palace doesn't stack.

cards.flavorText: It has slept underground for hundreds of millions of years since its birth. It's occasionally found during the excavation of caves.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Mimikyu

attacks.name: Gemstone Mimicry
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Tera Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Dottler

attacks.name: Disruptive Radar
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

cards.flavorText: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Orbeetle

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Rocket Brain
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Team Rocket's Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

cards.name: Mankey

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

cards.name: Primeape

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

cards.name: Annihilape

attacks.name: Impact Blow
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Impact Blow.

abilities.name: Lose Cool
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has 2 or more damage counters on it, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: When its anger rose beyond a critical point, this Pokémon gained power that is unfettered by the limits of its physical body.

cards.name: Ethan's Sudowoodo

attacks.name: Impound
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Try to Imitate
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

cards.flavorText: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears more similar to rock than to vegetation.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Larvitar

attacks.name: Mountain Munch
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Pupitar

attacks.name: Explosive Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: This pupa flies around wildly by venting with great force the gas pressurized inside its body.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Tyranitar

attacks.name: Demolition Tackle
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Sand Stream
abilities.text: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put 2 damage counters on each of your opponent's Basic Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn't care about others.

cards.name: Nosepass
cards.flavorText: The magnet in Nosepass's nose provides an unerring compass, making this Pokémon an excellent partner for Trainers going on a journey.

cards.name: Probopass

attacks.name: Mountain Drop
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 70 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force. With them, it can attack a foe from three directions.

cards.name: Meditite

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

cards.name: Medicham

attacks.name: Harmonious Spirit Palm
attacks.text: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

cards.name: Regirock ex

attacks.name: Regi Charge
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giant Rock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Cynthia's Gible

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

cards.flavorText: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

cards.name: Cynthia's Gabite

abilities.name: Champion's Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Cynthia's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

cards.name: Cynthia's Garchomp ex

attacks.name: Corkscrew Dive
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Draconic Buster
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Mudbray

attacks.name: Running Charge
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon covers itself in mud that it has regurgitated. The mud won't dry out even if it's exposed to the sun for a long time.

cards.name: Mudsdale

abilities.name: Mud Coat
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

cards.name: Arven's Toedscool

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.

cards.name: Arven's Toedscruel

attacks.name: Pull
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.

cards.name: Cornerstone Mask Ogerpon

attacks.name: Rock Kagura
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Fighting Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Mountain Ramming
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: This form has excellent defenses, absorbing impacts solidly like the cornerstones that support houses.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Ekans

attacks.name: Drag Down
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It can freely detach its jaw to swallow large prey whole. It can become too heavy to move, however.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Arbok

attacks.name: Spinning Tail
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Potent Glare
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Pokémon that has an Ability from their hand, except for Team Rocket's Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The pattern on its belly appears to be a frightening face. Weak foes will flee just at the sight of the pattern.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Nidoran♀

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: It uses its hard incisor teeth to crush and eat berries. The tip of a female Nidoran's horn is a bit more rounded than the tip of a male's horn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Nidorina

attacks.name: Dark Awakening
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 of your Darkness Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: If the group is threatened, these Pokémon will band together to assault enemies with a chorus of ultrasonic waves.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Nidoqueen

attacks.name: Love Impact
attacks.text: If a Pokémon that has "Nidoking" in its name is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It pacifies offspring by placing them in the gaps between the spines on its back. The spines will never secrete poison while young are present.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Nidoran♂
cards.flavorText: Small but brave, this Pokémon will hold its ground and even risk its life in battle to protect the female it's friendly with.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Nidorino

attacks.name: Horn Rend
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It's nervous and quick to act aggressively. The potency of its poison increases along with the level of adrenaline present in its body.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Nidoking ex

attacks.name: Tainted Horn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Zubat

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: Zubat live in caves, down where the sun's light won't reach. In the morning, they gather together to keep each other warm as they sleep.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Golbat

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

abilities.name: Sneaky Bite
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its feet are tiny, but this Pokémon walks skillfully. It sneaks up on sleeping prey before sinking in its fangs and slurping up blood.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Crobat ex

attacks.name: Assassin's Return
attacks.text: You may put this Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to this Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Biting Spree
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Grimer

attacks.name: Corrosive Sludge
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard the Defending Pokémon and all attached cards.

cards.flavorText: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Muk

attacks.name: Gooped Up
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Hazardous Venom
attacks.text: This attack does 100 damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It's thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Koffing

abilities.name: Smog Signals
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon that have "Koffing" in their name and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Toxic gas is held within its thin, balloon-shaped body, so it can cause massive explosions.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Weezing

attacks.name: Explode Together Now
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon in play that has "Koffing" or "Weezing" in its name (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.flavorText: Top-grade perfume is made using its internal poison gases by diluting them to the highest level.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Murkrow

attacks.name: Deceit
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

cards.flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Sneasel

attacks.name: Strike the Sleeper
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon for each damage counter on that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

cards.name: Cynthia's Spiritomb

attacks.name: Raging Curse
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Cynthia's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

cards.flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

cards.name: Marnie's Purrloin

attacks.name: Pointy Nails
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 40 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

cards.name: Marnie's Liepard

attacks.name: Pointy Claws
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This stealthy Pokémon sneaks up behind prey without making any sound at all. It competes with Thievul for territory.

cards.name: Marnie's Scraggy

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It stretches its saggy skin up to its neck to protect itself. Among Scraggy, individuals with saggier skin apparently garner more respect.

cards.name: Marnie's Scrafty

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

cards.name: Marnie's Impidimp

attacks.name: Filch
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: The reason this Pokémon causes trouble for those it feels close to is because Impidimp itself gets irritable if it can't absorb negative emotions.

cards.name: Marnie's Morgrem
cards.flavorText: Morgrem prefers dirty tactics, like ambushing, because it doesn't have confidence in its brawn.

cards.name: Marnie's Grimmsnarl ex

attacks.name: Shadow Bullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Punk Up
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 5 Basic Darkness Energy cards and attach them to your Marnie's Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Marnie's Morpeko

attacks.name: Spiky Wheel
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Morpeko eats seeds constantly to keep its hunger at bay. If it's hungry for too long, it grows wild and brutish.

cards.name: Arven's Maschiff
cards.flavorText: It always scowls in an attempt to make opponents take it seriously, but even crying children will burst into laughter when they see Maschiff's face.

cards.name: Arven's Mabosstiff ex

attacks.name: Vigorous Tackle
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: Boss Headbutt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Boss Headbutt.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Forretress

attacks.name: Iron Shake-Up
attacks.text: You may move any amount of Metal Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Hurricane of Needles
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It's usually found hanging on to a fat tree trunk. It shoots out bits of its shell when it sees action.

cards.name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Roost
attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: Its sturdy, heavy-looking iron body is actually thin and light, so it can fly at speeds over 180 mph.

cards.name: Steven's Skarmory

attacks.name: Sonic Double
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Its sturdy, heavy-looking iron body is actually thin and light, so it can fly at speeds over 180 mph.

cards.name: Steven's Beldum
cards.flavorText: The magnetic force generated by its body repels the ground's natural magnetism, letting it float.

cards.name: Steven's Metang

attacks.name: Metal Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its two brains are linked, amplifying its psychic power.

cards.name: Steven's Metagross ex

abilities.name: X-Boot
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Basic Psychic Energy card, a Basic Metal Energy card, or 1 of each and attach them to your Psychic Pokémon and Metal Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Zamazenta

attacks.name: Strong Bash
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Rattata

attacks.name: Dangerous Incisors
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is common but hazardous. Its sharp incisors can easily cut right through hard wood.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Raticate

attacks.name: Reckless Abandon
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Its whiskers are essential for maintaining its balance. No matter how friendly you are, it will get angry and bite you if you touch its whiskers.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Meowth

attacks.name: Paw-cket Pilfer
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Persian ex

attacks.name: Haughty Order
attacks.text: Reveal the top 10 cards of your opponent's deck. You may choose an attack from a Pokémon you find there and use it as this attack. Shuffle the revealed cards into your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Cruel Slash
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Dizzy Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.

cards.name: Tauros

attacks.name: Clean Hit
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

cards.flavorText: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Porygon

attacks.name: Hacking
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, your opponent discards a card from their hand.

cards.flavorText: It is an artificial Pokémon. Since it doesn't breathe, people are excited by its potential to be useful in any environment.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Porygon2

attacks.name: R Command
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name in your discard pile.

cards.flavorText: This artificial Pokémon evolved thanks to cutting- edge science. It sometimes displays behavior that is not in its programming.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Porygon-Z

attacks.name: R Command
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name in your discard pile.

abilities.name: Reconstitute
abilities.text: You must discard 2 cards from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

cards.flavorText: An additional program was installed to create a more advanced Pokémon, but it was apparently flawed. Porygon-Z moves oddly as a result.

cards.name: Taillow
cards.flavorText: It dislikes cold seasons. They migrate to other lands in search of warmth, flying over 180 miles a day.

cards.name: Swellow

attacks.name: Add On
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

cards.flavorText: It dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. It catches its prey with sharp claws.

cards.name: Arven's Skwovet

attacks.name: Gnaw Through
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It stores berries in its cheeks. When there are no berries to be found, Skwovet will stuff pebbles into its cheeks to stave off its cravings.

cards.name: Arven's Greedent

abilities.name: Greedy Order
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put up to 2 Arven's Sandwich cards from your discard pile into your hand.

cards.flavorText: If it spots a berry tree, it will immediately go to gather berries without a sideways glance—even if it's in the middle of a battle.

cards.name: Squawkabilly

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: You may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon prefer to live in cities. They form flocks based on the color of their feathers, and they fight over territory.

cards.name: Arven's Sandwich
rules: Heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon. If that Pokémon is an Arven's Pokémon, heal 100 damage from it instead.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Cynthia's Power Weight
rules: The Cynthia's Pokémon this card is attached to gets +70 HP.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Emcee's Hype
rules: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Energy Recycler
rules: Shuffle up to 5 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Ethan's Adventure
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Ethan's Pokémon and Basic Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Granite Cave
rules: Steven's Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) take 30 less damage from attacks from the opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Sacred Ash
rules: Shuffle up to 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Spikemuth Gym
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for a Marnie's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Archer
rules: You can use this card only if any of your Team Rocket's Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.  Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Ariana
rules: Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If all of your Pokémon in play are Team Rocket's Pokémon, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Bother-Bot
rules: Turn 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards face up and choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card. You may have your opponent switch those cards. (That Prize card remains face up for the rest of the game.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Factory
rules: Once during each player's turn, if they played a Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name from their hand this turn, they may draw 2 cards.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Giovanni
rules: Switch your Active Team Rocket's Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Team Rocket's Pokémon. If you do, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Great Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for an Evolution Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. If tails, search your deck for a Basic Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Petrel
rules: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Proton
rules: If you go first, you may use this card during your first turn.  Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Transceiver
rules: Search your deck for a Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Venture Bomb
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, put 2 damage counters on your Active Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Watchtower
rules: Colorless Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Energy
rules: This card can only be attached to a Team Rocket's Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Team Rocket's Pokémon, discard this card.  As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides 2 in any combination of Psychic Energy and Darkness Energy.

cards.name: Yanma

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: As Yanma surveys its territory, it periodically stops to hover in place by flapping its wings at high speeds.

cards.name: Cynthia's Roserade

abilities.name: Cheer On to Glory
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Cynthia's Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: The poison in its right hand is quick acting. The poison in its left hand is slow acting. Both are life threatening.

cards.name: Shaymin

abilities.name: Flower Curtain
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

cards.flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

cards.name: Crustle

attacks.name: Superb Scissors
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Mysterious Rock Inn
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.

cards.flavorText: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They're mighty enough to crack Rhyperior's carapace.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Spidops

attacks.name: Rocket Rush
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

abilities.name: Charging Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

cards.name: Hydrapple

attacks.name: Hydra Breath
attacks.text: Discard 6 Basic Grass Energy cards from your hand, and Knock Out your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you can't discard 6 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Seven syrpents live inside an apple made of syrup. The syrpent in the center is the commander.

cards.name: Rapidash

abilities.name: Hurried Gait
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

cards.flavorText: The fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd's pace and direction of travel.

cards.name: Ethan's Typhlosion

attacks.name: Buddy Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each Ethan's Adventure card in your discard pile.

cards.flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur to cause huge explosions.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Houndoom

attacks.name: Cruel Coal
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

cards.name: Blaziken

attacks.name: Inferno Kick Flurry
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

cards.name: Misty's Psyduck

abilities.name: Flustered Leap
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may discard the bottom card of your deck. If you do, discard all cards from this Pokémon and put this Pokémon on top of your deck.

cards.flavorText: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

cards.name: Misty's Lapras

attacks.name: Swim Together
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Misty's Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It ferries people across the sea on its back. It may sing an enchanting cry if it is in a good mood.

cards.name: Clamperl

attacks.name: Shell Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Clamperl's pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder's pearls.

cards.name: Electrike
cards.flavorText: It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.

cards.name: Rotom

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Orbeetle

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Rocket Brain
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Team Rocket's Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Weezing

attacks.name: Explode Together Now
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon in play that has "Koffing" or "Weezing" in its name (both yours and your opponent's).

cards.flavorText: Top-grade perfume is made using its internal poison gases by diluting them to the highest level.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Murkrow

attacks.name: Deceit
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

cards.flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

cards.name: Zamazenta

attacks.name: Strong Bash
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Raticate

attacks.name: Reckless Abandon
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Its whiskers are essential for maintaining its balance. No matter how friendly you are, it will get angry and bite you if you touch its whiskers.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Meowth

attacks.name: Paw-cket Pilfer
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

cards.name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Dizzy Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.

cards.name: Arven's Greedent

abilities.name: Greedy Order
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put up to 2 Arven's Sandwich cards from your discard pile into your hand.

cards.flavorText: If it spots a berry tree, it will immediately go to gather berries without a sideways glance—even if it's in the middle of a battle.

cards.name: Yanmega ex

attacks.name: Jet Cyclone
attacks.text: Move 3 Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Buzzing Boost
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may search your deck for up to 3 Basic Grass Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Arboliva ex

attacks.name: Oil Salvo
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon 6 times. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) For each time you chose a Pokémon, do 20 damage to it. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Aroma Shot
attacks.text: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Moltres ex

attacks.name: Flame Screen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Evil Incineration
attacks.text: Discard a Team Rocket's Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard your opponent's Active Pokémon and all attached cards.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Ethan's Ho-Oh ex

attacks.name: Shining Feathers
attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from each of your Pokémon.

abilities.name: Golden Flame
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic Fire Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Ethan's Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Cetitan ex

attacks.name: Crushing Press
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play. If you do, this attack does 140 more damage.

abilities.name: Snow Camouflage
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Dondozo ex

attacks.name: Avenging Billow
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamic Dive
attacks.text: You may do 120 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Electivire ex

attacks.name: Dual Bolt
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: High-Voltage Press
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Mewtwo ex

attacks.name: Erasure Ball
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

abilities.name: Power Saver
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't attack unless you have 4 or more Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Regirock ex

attacks.name: Regi Charge
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giant Rock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Cynthia's Garchomp ex

attacks.name: Corkscrew Dive
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Draconic Buster
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Nidoking ex

attacks.name: Tainted Horn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Crobat ex

attacks.name: Assassin's Return
attacks.text: You may put this Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to this Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Biting Spree
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Arven's Mabosstiff ex

attacks.name: Vigorous Tackle
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: Boss Headbutt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Boss Headbutt.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Biting Spree
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Levincia
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may put up to 2 Basic Lightning Energy cards from their discard pile into their hand.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Sewaddle
cards.flavorText: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

cards.name: Snivy
cards.flavorText: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

cards.name: Swadloon

abilities.name: Healing Leaves
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

cards.name: Servine

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

cards.name: Leavanny

attacks.name: Healing Wrapping
attacks.text: Heal 100 damage from each of your Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

cards.name: Serperior ex

attacks.name: Command the Grass
attacks.text: You may search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

abilities.name: Regal Cheer
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Pokémon do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Cottonee

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

cards.name: Pansage

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

cards.name: Whimsicott ex

attacks.name: Energy Gift
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wondrous Cotton
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Simisage
cards.flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

cards.name: Deerling
cards.flavorText: The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass.

cards.name: Petilil

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body.

cards.name: Sawsbuck

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck's horns.

cards.name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Bemusing Aroma
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

cards.name: Shelmet

abilities.name: Stimulated Evolution
abilities.text: If you have Karrablast in play, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

cards.flavorText: When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

cards.name: Maractus

attacks.name: Lively Needles
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

cards.name: Accelgor

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.

cards.name: Karrablast

abilities.name: Stimulated Evolution
abilities.text: If you have Shelmet in play, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

cards.flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

cards.name: Virizion

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Emerald Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

cards.name: Foongus

attacks.name: Toxic Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

cards.name: Tepig
cards.flavorText: It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

cards.name: Amoonguss

attacks.name: Dangerous Reaction
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

cards.name: Pignite
cards.flavorText: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

cards.name: Victini

attacks.name: V-Force
attacks.text: If you have 4 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

cards.name: Emboar

abilities.name: Inferno Fandango
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

cards.name: Darumaka
cards.flavorText: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep.

cards.name: Pansear

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.

cards.name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Smashing Headbutt
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its internal fire burns at 2,500° F, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.

cards.name: Simisear
cards.flavorText: It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

cards.name: Larvesta

attacks.name: Peck Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

cards.name: Litwick

attacks.name: Brighten and Burn
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.

cards.flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

cards.name: Volcarona

abilities.name: Torrid Scales
abilities.text: You must discard a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned.

cards.flavorText: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona's fire provided a replacement for the sun.

cards.name: Lampent

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

cards.name: Panpour

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

cards.name: Chandelure

attacks.name: Incendiary Pillar
attacks.text: If you have 10 or more Basic Fire Energy cards in your discard pile, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Burn It All Up
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
