cards.flavorText: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

cards.name: Simipour
cards.flavorText: The tuft on its head holds water. When the level runs low, it replenishes the tuft by siphoning up water with its tail.

cards.name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Licking Catch
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Fire Pokémon and Basic Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It breathes through a hole in its tail while it burns with an internal fire. Durant is its prey.

cards.name: Tympole

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

cards.flavorText: They warn others of danger by vibrating their cheeks to create a high-pitched sound.

cards.name: Reshiram ex

attacks.name: Blazing Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Palpitoad

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

cards.flavorText: When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

cards.name: Oshawott
cards.flavorText: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

cards.name: Seismitoad

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

cards.flavorText: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

cards.name: Dewott

attacks.name: Energized Shell
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Strict training is how it learns its flowing double-scalchop technique.

cards.name: Tirtouga

attacks.name: Ancient Seaweed
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Item card in your opponent's discard pile.

cards.flavorText: Restored from a fossil, this Pokémon can dive to depths beyond half a mile.

cards.name: Samurott

attacks.name: Energized Slash
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Torrential Whirlpool
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: One swing of the sword incorporated in its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them quiets everybody.

cards.name: Carracosta

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

abilities.name: Mighty Shell
abilities.text: Prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached.

cards.flavorText: They can live both in the ocean and on land. A slap from one of them is enough to open a hole in the bottom of a tanker.

cards.name: Basculin

attacks.name: Bared Fangs
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they'll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.

cards.name: Alomomola

abilities.name: Gentle Fin
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may put a Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less from your discard pile onto your Bench.

cards.flavorText: The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

cards.name: Ducklett

attacks.name: Firefighting
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food--peat moss.

cards.name: Cubchoo

attacks.name: Snotted Up
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases.

cards.name: Swanna

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

cards.name: Beartic

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

cards.name: Vanillite

attacks.name: Ice Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

cards.name: Cryogonal

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 20 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When its body temperature goes up, it turns into steam and vanishes. When its temperature lowers, it returns to ice.

cards.name: Vanillish

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon's habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

cards.name: Kyurem ex

attacks.name: Blizzard Burst
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Vanilluxe

attacks.name: Double Freeze
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads. If either of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.

cards.name: Emolga

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: The energy made in its cheeks' electric pouches is stored inside its membrane and released while it is gliding.

cards.name: Keldeo ex

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

cards.name: Blitzle
cards.flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use their flashing manes to communicate with one another.

cards.name: Eelektrik

abilities.name: Dynamotor
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

cards.name: Zebstrika

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

cards.name: Eelektross

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Buzz Flip
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

cards.name: Joltik

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses.

cards.name: Thundurus

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Disaster Volt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Countless charred remains mar the landscape of places through which Thundurus has passed.

cards.name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

cards.flavorText: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

cards.name: Zekrom ex

attacks.name: Voltage Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Muddy Bolt
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fighting Energy attached, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

cards.name: Munna

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.

cards.flavorText: Munna always float in the air. People whose dreams are eaten by them forget what the dreams had been about.

cards.name: Woobat
cards.flavorText: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

cards.name: Musharna

attacks.name: Dream Calling
attacks.text: You may search your deck for any number of Fennel cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

cards.flavorText: The mist emanating from their foreheads is packed with the dreams of people and Pokémon.

cards.name: Swoobat

attacks.name: Happy Return
attacks.text: Put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

cards.flavorText: It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks.

cards.name: Solosis
cards.flavorText: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

cards.name: Sigilyph

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

cards.flavorText: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

cards.name: Duosion

attacks.name: Cellular Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

cards.name: Yamask

attacks.name: Focused Wish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

cards.name: Reuniclus

attacks.name: Cellular Ascension
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Evo-Lariat
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Evolution Pokémon in play.

cards.flavorText: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

cards.name: Cofagrigus

attacks.name: Extended Damagriiigus
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

cards.name: Elgyem

attacks.name: Slight Shift
attacks.text: Move an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of their Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent's brain, causing unendurable headaches.

cards.name: Gothita
cards.flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

cards.name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It can manipulate an opponent's memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers.

cards.name: Gothorita

attacks.name: Fortunate Eye
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

cards.flavorText: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

cards.name: Golett

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

cards.name: Gothitelle

attacks.name: Synchro Shot
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.

abilities.name: Distorted Future
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may have your opponent shuffle their hand into their deck and draw 3 cards.

cards.flavorText: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

cards.name: Golurk

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

cards.name: Frillish

attacks.name: Oceanic Gloom
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

cards.flavorText: With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor.

cards.name: Meloetta ex

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 80 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

abilities.name: Debut Performance
abilities.text: If you go first, this Pokémon can use attacks during your first turn.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Jellicent ex

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 80 more damage.

abilities.name: Oceanic Curse
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Item cards or Pokémon Tool cards from their hand.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Drilbur
cards.flavorText: It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It could give a car running aboveground a good race.

cards.name: Roggenrola

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 40 or less.

cards.flavorText: Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

cards.name: Excadrill ex

attacks.name: Piercing Drill
attacks.text: This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Boldore

attacks.name: Smack Down
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has Fighting Resistance, this attack does 50 more damage.

cards.flavorText: When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves.

cards.name: Timburr
cards.flavorText: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty.

cards.name: Gigalith

attacks.name: Vengeful Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Fighting Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.

cards.name: Gurdurr

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

cards.name: Sawk

attacks.name: Rising Chop
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't a Pokémon ex, this attack does nothing. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

cards.flavorText: The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train.

cards.name: Conkeldurr

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

abilities.name: Craftsmanship
abilities.text: This Pokémon gets +40 HP for each Fighting Energy attached to it.

cards.flavorText: It is thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

cards.name: Archen

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another.

cards.name: Throh

attacks.name: Shoulder Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 30 less damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

cards.flavorText: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

cards.name: Archeops

abilities.name: Ancient Wing
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may devolve 1 of your opponent's evolved Pokémon by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

cards.name: Dwebble

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth.

cards.name: Mienfoo
cards.flavorText: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

cards.name: Crustle

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

cards.flavorText: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle.

cards.name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Smash Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

cards.flavorText: It wields the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

cards.name: Landorus

attacks.name: Abundant Harvest
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

cards.name: Terrakion

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

cards.name: Venipede

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

cards.name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Invite Evil
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: They steal from people for fun, but their victims can't help but forgive them. Their deceptively cute act is perfect.

cards.name: Whirlipede

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

cards.name: Liepard

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

cards.name: Scolipede

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

cards.name: Scraggy
cards.flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

cards.name: Sandile

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards a card from their hand.

cards.flavorText: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

cards.name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Ruffians Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Darkness Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

cards.name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

cards.flavorText: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

cards.name: Trubbish
cards.flavorText: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

cards.name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Cursed Slug
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

cards.name: Garbodor

attacks.name: Gunk Shot
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

cards.name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Wild Lances
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

cards.name: Zorua

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

cards.name: Klink

attacks.name: Hard Gears
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

cards.name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Foul Play
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

cards.flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

cards.name: Klang

attacks.name: Hard Gears
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: By changing the direction in which it rotates, it communicates its feelings to others. When angry, it rotates faster.

cards.name: Vullaby
cards.flavorText: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

cards.name: Klinklang

abilities.name: Gear Coating
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon that have any Metal Energy attached take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

cards.name: Mandibuzz

abilities.name: Look for Prey
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Your opponent reveals their hand, and you put a Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less that you find there onto your opponent's Bench.

cards.flavorText: It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.

cards.name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: Blades comprise this Pokémon's entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

cards.name: Deino

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

cards.name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Finishing Blow
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

cards.name: Zweilous

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

cards.name: Cobalion

attacks.name: Righteous Edge
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Arms
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

cards.name: Hydreigon ex

attacks.name: Dark Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

abilities.name: Greedy Eater
abilities.text: If your opponent's Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Genesect ex

attacks.name: Protect Charge
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

abilities.name: Metallic Signal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Evolution Metal Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Ferroseed
cards.flavorText: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

cards.name: Axew

attacks.name: Gather Strength
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

cards.name: Ferrothorn

attacks.name: Power Whip
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

cards.name: Fraxure

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they've been fighting.

cards.name: Durant

attacks.name: Bite Together
attacks.text: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

cards.name: Haxorus

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Axe Blast
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.

cards.flavorText: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel.

cards.name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

cards.name: Pidove

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

cards.flavorText: Each follows its Trainer's orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

cards.name: Patrat

attacks.name: Procurement
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

cards.name: Tranquill

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It can return to its Trainer's location regardless of the distance separating them.

cards.name: Watchog

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Hyper Fang attack's base damage is 240.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

cards.name: Unfezant

attacks.name: Add On
attacks.text: Draw 4 cards.

attacks.name: Swift Flight
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Males swing their head plumage to threaten opponents. The females' flying abilities surpass those of the males.

cards.name: Lillipup

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

cards.name: Audino

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

cards.flavorText: It touches others with the feelers on its ears, using the sound of their heartbeats to tell how they are feeling.

cards.name: Herdier

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It has black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

cards.name: Minccino

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

cards.name: Stoutland

attacks.name: Odor Sleuth
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Special Fang
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

cards.name: Cinccino

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

cards.name: Bouffalant ex

attacks.name: Gold Breaker
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.

abilities.name: Bouffer
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Rufflet
cards.flavorText: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

cards.name: Tornadus

attacks.name: Wrapped in Wind
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.text: Move a Basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

cards.name: Braviary

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

cards.flavorText: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

cards.name: Antique Plume Fossil

abilities.name: Plume Protection
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

rules: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.  At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Air Balloon
rules: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is ColorlessColorless less.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

cards.name: Brave Bangle
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn't have a Rule Box, the attacks it uses do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

cards.name: Cheren
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Energy Coin
rules: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, search your deck for a Basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Energy Retrieval
rules: Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Fennel
rules: Heal 40 damage from each of your Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Harlequin
rules: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards. If tails, you draw 3 cards, and your opponent draws 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: N's Plan
rules: Move up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Hilda
rules: Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Pokégear 3.0
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Supporter card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Tool Scrapper
rules: Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to Pokémon (yours or your opponent's) and discard them.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Ignition Energy
rules: If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.  As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  If this card is attached to an Evolution Pokémon, it provides ColorlessColorlessColorless Energy instead.

cards.name: Prism Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  If this card is attached to a Basic Pokémon, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

cards.name: Sewaddle
cards.flavorText: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

cards.name: Snivy
cards.flavorText: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

cards.name: Swadloon

abilities.name: Healing Leaves
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

cards.name: Servine

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

cards.name: Leavanny

attacks.name: Healing Wrapping
attacks.text: Heal 100 damage from each of your Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

cards.name: Pansage

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

cards.name: Cottonee

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

cards.name: Simisage
cards.flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

cards.name: Deerling
cards.flavorText: The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass.

cards.name: Petilil

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body.

cards.name: Sawsbuck

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck's horns.

cards.name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Bemusing Aroma
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

cards.name: Shelmet

abilities.name: Stimulated Evolution
abilities.text: If you have Karrablast in play, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

cards.flavorText: When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

cards.name: Maractus

attacks.name: Lively Needles
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

cards.name: Accelgor

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.

cards.name: Karrablast

abilities.name: Stimulated Evolution
abilities.text: If you have Shelmet in play, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

cards.flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

cards.name: Virizion

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Emerald Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

cards.name: Foongus

attacks.name: Toxic Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

cards.name: Tepig
cards.flavorText: It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

cards.name: Amoonguss

attacks.name: Dangerous Reaction
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

cards.name: Pignite
cards.flavorText: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

cards.name: Darumaka
cards.flavorText: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep.

cards.name: Emboar

abilities.name: Inferno Fandango
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

cards.name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Smashing Headbutt
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its internal fire burns at 2,500° F, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.

cards.name: Pansear

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.

cards.name: Larvesta

attacks.name: Peck Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

cards.name: Simisear
cards.flavorText: It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

cards.name: Volcarona

abilities.name: Torrid Scales
abilities.text: You must discard a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned.

cards.flavorText: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona's fire provided a replacement for the sun.

cards.name: Litwick

attacks.name: Brighten and Burn
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.

cards.flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

cards.name: Panpour

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

cards.name: Lampent

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

cards.name: Simipour
cards.flavorText: The tuft on its head holds water. When the level runs low, it replenishes the tuft by siphoning up water with its tail.

cards.name: Chandelure

attacks.name: Incendiary Pillar
attacks.text: If you have 10 or more Basic Fire Energy cards in your discard pile, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Burn It All Up
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

cards.name: Tympole

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

cards.flavorText: They warn others of danger by vibrating their cheeks to create a high-pitched sound.

cards.name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Licking Catch
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Fire Pokémon and Basic Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It breathes through a hole in its tail while it burns with an internal fire. Durant is its prey.

cards.name: Palpitoad

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

cards.flavorText: When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

cards.name: Oshawott
cards.flavorText: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

cards.name: Seismitoad

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

cards.flavorText: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

cards.name: Dewott

attacks.name: Energized Shell
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Strict training is how it learns its flowing double-scalchop technique.

cards.name: Tirtouga

attacks.name: Ancient Seaweed
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Item card in your opponent's discard pile.

cards.flavorText: Restored from a fossil, this Pokémon can dive to depths beyond half a mile.

cards.name: Samurott

attacks.name: Energized Slash
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Torrential Whirlpool
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: One swing of the sword incorporated in its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them quiets everybody.

cards.name: Carracosta

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

abilities.name: Mighty Shell
abilities.text: Prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached.

cards.flavorText: They can live both in the ocean and on land. A slap from one of them is enough to open a hole in the bottom of a tanker.

cards.name: Basculin

attacks.name: Bared Fangs
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they'll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.

cards.name: Alomomola

abilities.name: Gentle Fin
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may put a Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less from your discard pile onto your Bench.

cards.flavorText: The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

cards.name: Ducklett

attacks.name: Firefighting
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food--peat moss.

cards.name: Cubchoo

attacks.name: Snotted Up
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases.

cards.name: Swanna

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

cards.name: Beartic

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

cards.name: Vanillite

attacks.name: Ice Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

cards.name: Cryogonal

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 20 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When its body temperature goes up, it turns into steam and vanishes. When its temperature lowers, it returns to ice.

cards.name: Vanillish

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon's habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

cards.name: Emolga

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: The energy made in its cheeks' electric pouches is stored inside its membrane and released while it is gliding.

cards.name: Vanilluxe

attacks.name: Double Freeze
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads. If either of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.

cards.name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

cards.name: Blitzle
cards.flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use their flashing manes to communicate with one another.

cards.name: Eelektrik

abilities.name: Dynamotor
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

cards.name: Zebstrika

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

cards.name: Eelektross

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Buzz Flip
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

cards.name: Joltik

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses.

cards.name: Munna

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.

cards.flavorText: Munna always float in the air. People whose dreams are eaten by them forget what the dreams had been about.

cards.name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

cards.flavorText: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

cards.name: Musharna

attacks.name: Dream Calling
attacks.text: You may search your deck for any number of Fennel cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

cards.flavorText: The mist emanating from their foreheads is packed with the dreams of people and Pokémon.

cards.name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Muddy Bolt
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fighting Energy attached, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

cards.name: Solosis
cards.flavorText: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

cards.name: Woobat
cards.flavorText: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

cards.name: Duosion

attacks.name: Cellular Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

cards.name: Swoobat

attacks.name: Happy Return
attacks.text: Put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

cards.flavorText: It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks.

cards.name: Elgyem

attacks.name: Slight Shift
attacks.text: Move an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of their Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent's brain, causing unendurable headaches.

cards.name: Sigilyph

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

cards.flavorText: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

cards.name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It can manipulate an opponent's memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers.

cards.name: Yamask

attacks.name: Focused Wish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

cards.name: Golett

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

cards.name: Cofagrigus

attacks.name: Extended Damagriiigus
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

cards.name: Golurk

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

cards.name: Gothita
cards.flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

cards.name: Drilbur
cards.flavorText: It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It could give a car running aboveground a good race.

cards.name: Gothorita

attacks.name: Fortunate Eye
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

cards.flavorText: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

cards.name: Timburr
cards.flavorText: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty.

cards.name: Frillish

attacks.name: Oceanic Gloom
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

cards.flavorText: With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor.

cards.name: Gurdurr

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

cards.name: Roggenrola

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 40 or less.

cards.flavorText: Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

cards.name: Conkeldurr

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

abilities.name: Craftsmanship
abilities.text: This Pokémon gets +40 HP for each Fighting Energy attached to it.

cards.flavorText: It is thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

cards.name: Boldore

attacks.name: Smack Down
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has Fighting Resistance, this attack does 50 more damage.

cards.flavorText: When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves.

cards.name: Throh

attacks.name: Shoulder Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 30 less damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

cards.flavorText: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

cards.name: Gigalith

attacks.name: Vengeful Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Fighting Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.

cards.name: Dwebble

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth.

cards.name: Sawk

attacks.name: Rising Chop
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't a Pokémon ex, this attack does nothing. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

cards.flavorText: The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train.

cards.name: Crustle

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

cards.flavorText: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle.

cards.name: Archen

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another.

cards.name: Landorus

attacks.name: Abundant Harvest
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

cards.name: Archeops

abilities.name: Ancient Wing
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may devolve 1 of your opponent's evolved Pokémon by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

cards.name: Venipede

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

cards.name: Mienfoo
cards.flavorText: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

cards.name: Whirlipede

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

cards.name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Smash Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

cards.flavorText: It wields the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

cards.name: Scolipede

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

cards.name: Terrakion

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

cards.name: Sandile

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards a card from their hand.

cards.flavorText: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

cards.name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Invite Evil
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: They steal from people for fun, but their victims can't help but forgive them. Their deceptively cute act is perfect.

cards.name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

cards.flavorText: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

cards.name: Liepard

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

cards.name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Cursed Slug
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

cards.name: Scraggy
cards.flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

cards.name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Wild Lances
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

cards.name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Ruffians Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Darkness Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

cards.name: Klink

attacks.name: Hard Gears
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

cards.name: Trubbish
cards.flavorText: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

cards.name: Klang

attacks.name: Hard Gears
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: By changing the direction in which it rotates, it communicates its feelings to others. When angry, it rotates faster.

cards.name: Garbodor

attacks.name: Gunk Shot
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

cards.name: Klinklang

abilities.name: Gear Coating
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon that have any Metal Energy attached take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

cards.name: Zorua

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

cards.name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: Blades comprise this Pokémon's entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

cards.name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Foul Play
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

cards.flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

cards.name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Finishing Blow
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

cards.name: Vullaby
cards.flavorText: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

cards.name: Cobalion

attacks.name: Righteous Edge
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Arms
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

cards.name: Mandibuzz

abilities.name: Look for Prey
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Your opponent reveals their hand, and you put a Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less that you find there onto your opponent's Bench.

cards.flavorText: It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.

cards.name: Axew

attacks.name: Gather Strength
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

cards.name: Deino

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

cards.name: Fraxure

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they've been fighting.

cards.name: Zweilous

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

cards.name: Haxorus

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Axe Blast
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.

cards.flavorText: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel.

cards.name: Ferroseed
cards.flavorText: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

cards.name: Pidove

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

cards.flavorText: Each follows its Trainer's orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

cards.name: Ferrothorn

attacks.name: Power Whip
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

cards.name: Tranquill

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It can return to its Trainer's location regardless of the distance separating them.

cards.name: Durant

attacks.name: Bite Together
attacks.text: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

cards.name: Unfezant

attacks.name: Add On
attacks.text: Draw 4 cards.

attacks.name: Swift Flight
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Males swing their head plumage to threaten opponents. The females' flying abilities surpass those of the males.

cards.name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

cards.name: Audino

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

cards.flavorText: It touches others with the feelers on its ears, using the sound of their heartbeats to tell how they are feeling.

cards.name: Patrat

attacks.name: Procurement
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

cards.name: Minccino

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

cards.name: Watchog

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Hyper Fang attack's base damage is 240.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

cards.name: Cinccino

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

cards.name: Lillipup

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

cards.name: Rufflet
cards.flavorText: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

cards.name: Herdier

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It has black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

cards.name: Braviary

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

cards.flavorText: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

cards.name: Stoutland

attacks.name: Odor Sleuth
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Special Fang
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

cards.name: Serperior ex

attacks.name: Command the Grass
attacks.text: You may search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

abilities.name: Regal Cheer
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Pokémon do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Whimsicott ex

attacks.name: Energy Gift
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wondrous Cotton
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Kyurem ex

attacks.name: Blizzard Burst
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Reshiram ex

attacks.name: Blazing Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Zekrom ex

attacks.name: Voltage Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Keldeo ex

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.
