rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may discard an Energy card from their hand in order to draw cards until they have as many cards in their hand as they have Psychic Pokémon in play.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Pokémon Center Lady
rules: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Premium Power Pro
rules: During this turn, attacks used by your Fighting Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Repel
rules: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Risky Ruins
rules: Whenever any player puts a Basic non-Darkness Pokémon onto their Bench during their turn, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Strange Timepiece
rules: Devolve 1 of your evolved Psychic Pokémon by putting any number of Evolution cards on it into your hand. (That Pokémon can't evolve this turn.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Surfing Beach
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may switch their Active Water Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Water Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Ultra Ball
rules: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.  Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Wally's Compassion
rules: Heal all damage from 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex. If you healed any damage in this way, put all Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Stomping Wood
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Fermented Juice
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has any Grass Energy attached, you may use this Ability. Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Cast-Off Shell
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Shedinja and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Aqua Launcher
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Fall Back to Reload
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from the Active Spot to your Bench, you may use this Ability. Attach up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bring Down
attacks.text: Choose a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent's) that has the least HP remaining, except for this Pokémon, and it is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Water Shot
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Damage Beat
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Fragile Husk
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon ex, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

attacks.name: Horrifying Bite
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

attacks.name: Shadowy Side Kick
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Powerful a-Salt
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Fighting Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Mountain Breaker
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

abilities.name: Spiteful Swirl
abilities.text: If your Active Darkness Pokémon is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if your Active Darkness Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Welcoming Tail
attacks.text: If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 200 more damage.

attacks.name: Pluck
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Quick Gift
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

abilities.name: Evidence Gathering
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.

cards.name: Mega Venusaur ex

attacks.name: Jungle Dump
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Solar Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may use this Ability. Move a Basic Grass Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Roasting Heat
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 160 more damage.

attacks.name: Volcanic Meteor
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.text: Discard the top 6 cards of your deck, and this attack does 100 damage for each Basic Water Energy card that you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Frost Barrier
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Riotous Blasting

rules: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is ColorlessColorless less.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Mega Brave.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Oddish
cards.flavorText: Its scientific name is Oddium wanderus. It is said to cover distances as far as 1,000 feet when night falls, walking on its two roots.

cards.name: Gloom

attacks.name: Disperse Drool
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: The fluid that oozes from its mouth isn't drool. It is a nectar that is used to attract prey.

cards.name: Vileplume

attacks.name: Pollen Bomb
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Lively Flower
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The bud bursts into bloom with a bang. It then starts scattering allergenic, poisonous pollen.

cards.name: Mega Heracross ex

attacks.name: Juggernaut Horn
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Mountain Ramming
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Lotad
cards.flavorText: The leaf on its head is naturally dirt repellent and will stay clean even after transporting Pokémon that are covered in mud.

cards.name: Lombre

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It prefers waterfronts with plentiful food. It became nocturnal so it wouldn't have to compete for food with bird Pokémon.

cards.name: Ludicolo

abilities.name: Excited Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have any Grass Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: There are structures throughout its whole body that produce energy when hit by sound waves with a cheerful rhythm.

cards.name: Genesect

attacks.name: Bug's Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

cards.name: Nymble

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it's in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

cards.name: Lokix

attacks.name: Jumping Shot
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

cards.flavorText: When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.

cards.name: Charmander

abilities.name: Agile
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

cards.flavorText: The flame on its tail shows the strength of its life-force. If Charmander is weak, the flame also burns weakly.

cards.name: Charmeleon
cards.flavorText: When it swings its burning tail, the temperature around it rises higher and higher, tormenting its opponents.

cards.name: Mega Charizard X ex

attacks.name: Inferno X
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Fire Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Moltres

attacks.name: Fighting Wings
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: There are stories of this Pokémon using its radiant, flame-cloaked wings to light up paths for those lost in the mountains.

cards.name: Darumaka

attacks.name: Blaze Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it's pushed or pulled.

cards.name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Blaze Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon's power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

cards.name: Reshiram

attacks.name: Burning Flare
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down.

cards.name: Oricorio ex

abilities.name: Excited Turbo
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, if you have any Fire Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Fire Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Charcadet

attacks.name: Gather Strength
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

cards.name: Ceruledge

attacks.name: Infernal Slash
attacks.text: Discard 4 Basic Fire Energy cards from your hand. If you can't discard 4 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

cards.name: Seel

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The protrusion on its head is very hard. It is used for bashing through thick ice.

cards.name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Fire or Water Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It sleeps under shallow ocean waters during the day, then looks for food at night when it's colder.

cards.name: Swinub
cards.flavorText: It searches for food by digging into the ground with its snout. Even frozen ground doesn't give it any trouble.

cards.name: Piloswine

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Covered by a shaggy coat, it is resistant to the cold. Its tusks of ice thicken when it snows.

cards.name: Mamoswine

attacks.name: Wreck
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.

attacks.name: Blizzard Edge
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: A frozen Mamoswine was dug from ice dating back 10,000 years. This Pokémon has been around a long, long, long time.

cards.name: Suicune

attacks.name: Crystal Fall
attacks.text: If you have at least 4 Water Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

cards.name: Piplup

attacks.name: Call for Support
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

cards.name: Prinplup

attacks.name: Targeted Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees in half with a single hit.

cards.name: Rotom ex

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Multi Adapter
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has "Rotom" in its name may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached. If this Ability goes away, discard Pokémon Tools from those Pokémon until only 1 remains on each.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Yamper

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

cards.name: Boltund

attacks.name: Electric Run
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

cards.name: Pawmi

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.

cards.name: Pawmo
cards.flavorText: When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

cards.name: Pawmot

attacks.name: Voltaic Fist
attacks.text: You may have this Pokémon also do 60 damage to itself and make your opponent's Active Pokémon Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

cards.name: Misdreavus
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

cards.name: Mismagius ex

attacks.name: Hexa-Magic
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

abilities.name: Swirling Prose
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent's Active Pokémon moves to the Bench during their turn, their new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Snubbull
cards.flavorText: In truth, it is a cowardly Pokémon. It growls eagerly in order to hide its fear from its opponent.

cards.name: Granbull

attacks.name: Finishing Blow
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It is actually timid and easily spooked. If attacked, it desperately flails its limbs about in an attempt to repel its opponent.

cards.name: Cresselia

attacks.name: Swelling Light
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Psychic Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

cards.name: Meloetta

attacks.name: Soothing Melody
attacks.text: Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Benched Psychic Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

cards.name: Mega Diancie ex

attacks.name: Garland Ray
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Energy cards from this Pokémon, and this attack does 120 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

abilities.name: Diamond Coat
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Mimikyu

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

cards.name: Milcery

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The more sweet aromas it absorbs, the more its body swells. Milcery will deflate when its energy level drops.

cards.name: Alcremie

attacks.name: Sweet Circle
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.

cards.flavorText: Desserts that Alcremie have decorated with their cream have a rich, sweet flavor and bring happiness to all who eat them.

cards.name: Zacian

attacks.name: Limit Break
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Able to cut down anything with a single strike, it became known as the Fairy King's Sword, and it inspired awe in friend and foe alike.

cards.name: Bramblin

attacks.name: Sneaky Placement
attacks.text: Place 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: A soul unable to move on to the afterlife was blown around by the wind until it got tangled up with dried grass and became a Pokémon.

cards.name: Brambleghast

abilities.name: Prison Panic
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Make your opponent's Active Pokémon Confused.

cards.flavorText: It will open the branches of its head to envelop its prey. Once it absorbs all the life energy it needs, it expels the prey and discards it.

cards.name: Paldean Tauros

attacks.name: Raging Charge
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon that has "Tauros" in its name that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon has a muscular body and excels at close-quarters combat. It uses its short horns to strike the opponent's weak spots.

cards.name: Gligar

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: It builds its nest on a steep cliff. When it is done gliding, it hops along the ground back to its nest.

cards.name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: If it succeeds in catching even a faint breeze properly, it can circle the globe without flapping once.

cards.name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon lives in arid deserts. It patiently awaits prey inside its funnel- shaped nest.

cards.name: Vibrava
cards.flavorText: Rather than using its underdeveloped wings for flight, it rubs them together, emitting ultrasonic waves to attack its enemies.

cards.name: Flygon

abilities.name: Sandy Flapping
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. You may also use this Ability if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon. Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: Known as the Desert Spirit, this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

cards.name: Gastly
cards.flavorText: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

cards.name: Haunter
cards.flavorText: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

cards.name: Mega Gengar ex

attacks.name: Void Gale
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Shadowy Concealment
abilities.text: If 1 of your Darkness Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon ex, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card. The effect of Shadowy Concealment doesn't stack.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

cards.name: Honchkrow

attacks.name: Sniping Feathers
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

cards.name: Sableye

attacks.name: Cocky Claw
attacks.text: If you have any Stage 2 Darkness Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 70 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

cards.name: Carvanha

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon have sharp fangs and powerful jaws. Sailors avoid Carvanha dens at all costs.

cards.name: Mega Sharpedo ex

attacks.name: Greedy Fang
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Hungry Jaws
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Seviper

abilities.name: Excited Power
abilities.text: If you have any Darkness Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It sharpens its swordlike tail on hard rocks. It hides in tall grass and strikes unwary prey with venomous fangs.

cards.name: Absol

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

cards.flavorText: Because of this Pokémon's ability to detect danger, people mistook Absol as a bringer of doom.

cards.name: Sandile
cards.flavorText: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

cards.name: Krokorok
cards.flavorText: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

cards.name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Vengeful Fang
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 160 more damage.

cards.flavorText: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

cards.name: Toxel

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel's body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.

cards.name: Toxtricity

abilities.name: Sinister Surge
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Basic Darkness Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Darkness Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: As it scatters toxic sweat and emits electricity, a melody that sounds like it came from a guitar reverberates through the surrounding area.

cards.name: Eternatus

attacks.name: Shatter
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Power Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.

cards.name: Empoleon ex

attacks.name: Iron Feathers
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

abilities.name: Emperor's Stance
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

cards.flavorText: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor's back contained a mysterious power.

cards.name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Tool Drop
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

cards.name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: When it's in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

cards.name: Duraludon

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Duraludon's body is comprised of a special metal that's lightweight and scratch resistant. It's also smooth, as though it was given a mirror finish.

cards.name: Archaludon

attacks.name: Coated Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.

cards.name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Ball Roll
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

cards.name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

cards.flavorText: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

cards.name: Aipom

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand, and your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

cards.flavorText: It lives atop tall trees. When leaping from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.

cards.name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Dual Tail
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 60 damage to each of 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: They live on large trees. They are said to communicate by connecting their tails to those of others.

cards.name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Energizing Sketch
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. Attach an amount of Basic Energy up to the number of heads from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

cards.flavorText: Once a Smeargle reaches adulthood, it will have other members of its species leave paw prints on its back.

cards.name: Zigzagoon

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

cards.name: Linoone

abilities.name: Excited Dash
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, and if you have any Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It uses its explosive speed and razor-sharp claws to bring down prey. Running along winding paths is not its strong suit.

cards.name: Buneary

attacks.name: Run Around
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them. This attack becomes stronger with training.

cards.name: Mega Lopunny ex

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 170 more damage.

attacks.name: Spiky Hopper
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Battle Cage
rules: Prevent all damage counters from being placed on Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) by effects of attacks and Abilities from the opponent's Pokémon. (Damage from attacks is still taken.)
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Blowtorch
rules: You can use this card only if you discard a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand.  Discard a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon, or discard a Stadium in play.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Dawn
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, a Stage 1 Pokémon, and a Stage 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Dizzying Valley
rules: Confused Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) don't recover from that Special Condition when they evolve or devolve.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

cards.name: Firebreather
rules: Search your deck for up to 7 Basic Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Grimsley's Move
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck and put a Darkness Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck. You can't use this card during your first turn.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Jumbo Ice Cream
rules: Heal 80 damage from your Active Pokémon that has 3 or more Energy attached.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Punk Helmet
rules: If the Darkness Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Sacred Charm
rules: The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have an Ability (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Wondrous Patch
rules: Attach a Basic Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Psychic Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Ludicolo

abilities.name: Excited Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have any Grass Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: There are structures throughout its whole body that produce energy when hit by sound waves with a cheerful rhythm.

cards.name: Nymble

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it's in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

cards.name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Fire or Water Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It sleeps under shallow ocean waters during the day, then looks for food at night when it's colder.

cards.name: Piplup

attacks.name: Call for Support
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

cards.name: Yamper

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

cards.name: Zacian

attacks.name: Limit Break
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Able to cut down anything with a single strike, it became known as the Fairy King's Sword, and it inspired awe in friend and foe alike.

cards.name: Flygon

abilities.name: Sandy Flapping
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. You may also use this Ability if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon. Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: Known as the Desert Spirit, this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

cards.name: Toxtricity

abilities.name: Sinister Surge
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Basic Darkness Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Darkness Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: As it scatters toxic sweat and emits electricity, a melody that sounds like it came from a guitar reverberates through the surrounding area.

cards.name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: When it's in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

cards.name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

cards.flavorText: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

cards.name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Dual Tail
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 60 damage to each of 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: They live on large trees. They are said to communicate by connecting their tails to those of others.

cards.name: Mega Heracross ex

attacks.name: Juggernaut Horn
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Mountain Ramming
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Mega Charizard X ex

attacks.name: Inferno X
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Fire Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Oricorio ex

abilities.name: Excited Turbo
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, if you have any Fire Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Fire Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Rotom ex

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Multi Adapter
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has "Rotom" in its name may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached. If this Ability goes away, discard Pokémon Tools from those Pokémon until only 1 remains on each.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Mismagius ex

attacks.name: Hexa-Magic
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

abilities.name: Swirling Prose
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent's Active Pokémon moves to the Bench during their turn, their new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Mega Sharpedo ex

attacks.name: Greedy Fang
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Hungry Jaws
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.

You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Ignition Energy
rules: If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.  As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  If this card is attached to an Evolution Pokémon, it provides ColorlessColorlessColorless Energy instead.

attacks.name: Inferno X
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Fire Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.name: Mega Charizard X ex

attacks.name: Inferno X
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Fire Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Dustox

attacks.name: Twilight Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

abilities.name: Boisterous Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Flip a coin. If heads, put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

cards.flavorText: A nocturnal Pokémon. Drawn by streetlights, they messily eat the leaves of trees lining boulevards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Tarountula

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon’s natural enemy.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Spidops

attacks.name: Rocket Rush
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

abilities.name: Charging Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

cards.name: Wurmple
cards.flavorText: It loves to eat leaves. If it is attacked by a Starly, it will defend itself with its spiked rear.

cards.name: Silcoon

abilities.name: Multiplying Cocoon
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Silcoon or a Cascoon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It wraps silk around the branches of a tree. It drinks rainwater on its silk while awaiting evolution.

cards.name: Beautifly

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Energy Straw
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand, and this attack does 80 damage for each Energy card you find there.

cards.flavorText: Vibrantly patterned wings are its prominent feature. It sucks sweet flower nectar with its long mouth.

cards.name: Cascoon

attacks.name: Trading Places
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its body, which is made of soft silk, hardens over time. When cracks appear, evolution is near.

cards.name: Ethan's Slugma
cards.flavorText: Its lava body can cool and chip away at times, but a magma bath will heal it right up.

cards.name: Numel

abilities.name: Incandescent Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: The magma in its body reaches 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit. Its hump gets smaller when it uses Fire-type moves.

cards.name: Ethan's Magcargo

attacks.name: Lava Burst
attacks.text: Discard up to 5 Fire Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

abilities.name: Melt Away
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon lives near the craters of volcanoes. It stores fire energy in its shell of cooled and hardened magma.

cards.name: Entei

attacks.name: Flare Fall
attacks.text: If you have at least 4 Fire Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: A Pokémon that races across the land. It is said that one is born every time a new volcano appears.

cards.name: Ethan's Ho-Oh ex

attacks.name: Shining Feathers
attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from each of your Pokémon.

abilities.name: Golden Flame
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic Fire Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Ethan's Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: N's Darumaka
cards.flavorText: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled.

cards.name: Camerupt

attacks.name: Roasting Burn
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't Burned, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Power Stomp

cards.name: Psyduck

abilities.text: Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) lose any Ability that requires the Pokémon using it to Knock Out itself.

cards.name: Glastrier

attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Frosty Typhoon
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Frosty Typhoon.

cards.name: Eelektrik

abilities.name: Dynamotor
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

cards.name: Mega Eelektross ex

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to each of 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Disaster Shock
attacks.text: You may discard 2 Lightning Energy from this Pokémon and make your opponent's Active Pokémon Paralyzed.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Exeggcute

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Using telepathy only fellow Exeggcute can pick up on, they always form a cluster of six.

cards.name: Mismagius

attacks.name: Assassin's Magic
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, place 6 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

cards.name: Cynthia's Gible

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

cards.flavorText: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

cards.name: Cynthia's Gabite

abilities.name: Champion's Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Cynthia's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

cards.name: Cynthia's Garchomp ex

attacks.name: Corkscrew Dive
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Draconic Buster
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Rolycoly
cards.flavorText: Chunks of the surface of this Pokémon’s body that have grown old and flaked off have long been used for fuel as an alternative to coal.

cards.name: Carkol

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Due to the coal tar created inside it, the heap of coal on Carkol’s back never falls apart, even when the Pokémon rolls around at high speeds.

cards.name: Coalossal

attacks.name: Tar Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 140 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If you don't have 10 or more Basic Fighting Energy cards in your discard pile, this attack does nothing. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bulky Bump
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It’s gentle usually but fearsome when angered. With a body that burns at over 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit, it crushes foes and turns them to ash.

cards.name: Okidogi

attacks.name: Settle the Score
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each Prize card your opponent took during their last turn.

cards.flavorText: Okidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.

cards.name: Gastly
cards.flavorText: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

cards.name: Cynthia's Spiritomb

attacks.name: Raging Curse
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Cynthia's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

cards.flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

cards.name: Pecharunt

attacks.name: Mochi Rush
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Mochi Rush attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

abilities.name: Final Chain
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Its peach-shaped shell serves as storage for a potent poison. It makes poisonous mochi and serves them to people and Pokémon.

cards.name: Dratini
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is full of life energy. It continually sheds its skin and grows steadily larger.

cards.name: Mega Dragonite ex

attacks.name: Ryuno Glide
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Sky Transport
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: N's Reshiram

attacks.name: Powerful Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down.

cards.name: Noibat

attacks.name: Knickknack Carrying
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves.

cards.name: Noivern

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Enhanced Blade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

cards.flavorText: They fly around on moonless nights and attack careless prey. Nothing can beat them in a battle in the dark.

cards.name: Hop's Cramorant

attacks.name: Fickle Spitting
attacks.text: If your opponent doesn't have exactly 3 or 4 Prize cards remaining, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: Cramorant instinctively swallow everything whole. They’re at their most formidable when they’re struggling to swallow overly large prey.

cards.name: Premium Power Pro
rules: During this turn, attacks used by your Fighting Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Tool Scrapper
rules: Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to Pokémon (yours or your opponent's) and discard them.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Energy
rules: This card can only be attached to a Team Rocket's Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Team Rocket's Pokémon, discard this card.  As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides 2 in any combination of Psychic Energy and Darkness Energy.

cards.name: Golduck

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Damp
abilities.text: Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) lose any Ability that requires the Pokémon using it to Knock Out itself.

cards.flavorText: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

cards.name: Snorunt
cards.flavorText: In some snowy lands, certain folklore says a house will prosper if a Snorunt lives there.

cards.name: Mega Froslass ex

attacks.name: Resentful Refrain
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Absolute Snow
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: N's Vanillite

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: The temperature of their breath is -58 degrees Fahrenheit. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

cards.name: Frosmoth

attacks.name: Cold Cyclone
attacks.text: Move a Water Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Alluring Wings
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Each player draws a card.

cards.flavorText: It causes blizzards as it flies around with its huge, chill-emanating wings. Clean meltwater is its favorite thing to drink.

cards.name: Heliolisk

attacks.name: Powerful Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

abilities.name: Frilled Generator
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you played Canari from your hand this turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Lightning Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

cards.name: Hop's Pincurchin ex

attacks.name: Spiky Thunder
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

abilities.name: Counterattack Quills
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Call Sign
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

cards.name: Shuppet
cards.flavorText: It loves to feed on feelings like envy and malice. Its upright horn catches the emotions of people.

cards.name: Rotom

attacks.name: Roto Call
attacks.text: You may search your deck for any number of Pokémon that have "Rotom" in their name and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

cards.name: Hop's Trevenant

attacks.name: Horrifying Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Hop's Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It’s kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body.

cards.name: Spectrier

attacks.name: Phantasmal Barrage
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon and place 12 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It probes its surroundings with all its senses save one—it doesn’t use its sense of sight. Spectrier’s kicks are said to separate soul from body.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Diglett

attacks.name: Relentless Burrowing
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: Its skin is very thin. If it is exposed to light, its blood heats up, causing it to grow weak.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Dugtrio

abilities.name: Holes
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent's Active Pokémon moves to the Bench during their turn, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: In battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.name: Riolu

attacks.name: Accelerating Stab
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Accelerating Stab.

cards.flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

cards.name: Mega Lucario ex

attacks.name: Aura Jab
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Mega Brave
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Mega Brave.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can’t help grinning if anyone pats its head.

cards.name: Mega Gengar ex

attacks.name: Void Gale
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Shadowy Concealment
abilities.text: If 1 of your Darkness Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon ex, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card. The effect of Shadowy Concealment doesn't stack.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: N's Zorua
cards.flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

cards.name: Rayquaza

attacks.name: Breakthrough Assault
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

cards.name: N's Zekrom

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rampaging Thunder
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings.

attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name from your hand during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Forest of Vitality
rules: Each player's Grass Pokémon can evolve into Grass Pokémon during the turn they play those Pokémon, except during their first turn.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

cards.name: N's PP Up
rules: Attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched N's Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Great Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for an Evolution Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. If tails, search your deck for a Basic Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Hypnotizer
rules: If the Team Rocket's Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Team Rocket's Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Asleep.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Petrel
rules: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Watchtower
rules: Colorless Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

cards.name: Thick Scale
rules: The Dragon Pokémon this card is attached to takes 50 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Grass, Fire, Water, or Lightning Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Prism Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  If this card is attached to a Basic Pokémon, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

cards.name: Beautifly

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Energy Straw
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand, and this attack does 80 damage for each Energy card you find there.

cards.flavorText: Vibrantly patterned wings are its prominent feature. It sucks sweet flower nectar with its long mouth.

cards.name: Dustox

attacks.name: Twilight Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

abilities.name: Boisterous Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Flip a coin. If heads, put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

cards.flavorText: A nocturnal Pokémon. Drawn by streetlights, they messily eat the leaves of trees lining boulevards.

cards.name: N's Vanillish

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

cards.flavorText: They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.

cards.name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

cards.name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Pouncing Trap
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat. During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 100 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.

cards.name: Helioptile
cards.flavorText: The frills on either side of its head have cells that generate electricity when exposed to sunlight.

cards.name: Iono's Kilowattrel

abilities.name: Flashing Draw
abilities.text: You must discard a Basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

cards.flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

cards.name: Lillie's Clefairy ex

attacks.name: Full Moon Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

abilities.name: Fairy Zone
abilities.text: The Weakness of each of your opponent's Dragon Pokémon in play is now Psychic. (Apply Weakness as ×2.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tri Kinesis
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If all of them are heads, Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It is said that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Mewtwo ex

attacks.name: Erasure Ball
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

abilities.name: Power Saver
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't attack unless you have 4 or more Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It loves to bite and yank people's hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

cards.name: Ralts

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

cards.name: Mega Gardevoir ex

attacks.name: Overflowing Wishes
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Mega Symphonia
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Banette

attacks.name: Cursed Words
attacks.text: Your opponent chooses 3 cards from their hand and shuffles those cards into their deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Mimikyu

attacks.name: Gemstone Mimicry
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Tera Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

attacks.name: Seventh Kick
attacks.text: If you don't have exactly 7 cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.

cards.name: Lunatone

abilities.name: Lunar Cycle
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have Solrock in play, you may discard a Basic Fighting Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Draw 3 cards. You can't use more than 1 Lunar Cycle Ability each turn.

cards.flavorText: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

cards.name: Regirock ex

attacks.name: Regi Charge
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giant Rock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

cards.name: Mega Hawlucha ex

attacks.name: Somersault Dive
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 140 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.

abilities.name: Tenacious Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

cards.name: Haunter
cards.flavorText: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Murkrow

attacks.name: Deceit
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

cards.flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Honchkrow

attacks.name: Rocket Feathers
attacks.text: You may discard any number of Supporter cards that have "Team Rocket" in their name from your hand, and this attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

cards.flavorText: A single cry from this nocturnal Pokémon, and more than 100 of its Murkrow cronies will assemble.

cards.name: Galarian Zigzagoon
cards.flavorText: Its restlessness has it constantly running around. If it sees another Pokémon, it will purposely run into them in order to start a fight.

cards.name: Scraggy

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

cards.name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Any chips in its blades would prove fatal for it. After each battle, it diligently maintains its blades using its favorite sharpening stone.

cards.name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Rapid Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

cards.flavorText: Bisharp mercilessly cuts its opponents to pieces with the sharp blades covering its body. It will do anything to win.

cards.name: Kingambit

attacks.name: Double-Edged Slash
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

abilities.name: Supreme Overlord
abilities.text: Attacks used by this Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: Though it commands a massive army in battle, it’s not skilled at devising complex strategies. It just uses brute strength to keep pushing.

cards.name: Team Rocket's Meowth

attacks.name: Paw-cket Pilfer
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

cards.name: Air Balloon
rules: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is ColorlessColorless less.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Boss's Orders
rules: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Fighting Gong
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Fighting Energy card or a Basic Fighting Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

cards.name: Iris's Fighting Spirit
rules: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.  Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Light Ball
rules: Attacks used by the Pikachu ex this card is attached to do 50 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

cards.name: Lillie's Determination
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

cards.name: Mystery Garden
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may discard an Energy card from their hand in order to draw cards until they have as many cards in their hand as they have Psychic Pokémon in play.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.
